parameters.name: MetricCollectionEnabled
parameters.description: The MetricCollectionEnabled parameter indicates the desired operation to perform on the metrics.

parameters.name: Definition
parameters.description: The Definition parameter identifies a CIM_BaseMetricDefinition for which metrics will be returned.

parameters.name: Range
parameters.description: The Range parameter identifies how the instances are selected. The algorithm for ordering value instances is metric definition specific.

parameters.name: Count
parameters.description: The Count parameter identifies the maximum number of instances to to be returned by the method.

parameters.name: Values
parameters.description: Upon successful completion of the method, the Values parameter contains references to instances of CIM_BaseMetricValue, filteredaccording to the values of the input parameters.

parameters.name: StartSampleTime
parameters.description:
Point in time when sampling for the metrics is to be started. 
A value of 99990101000000.000000+000 shall indicate that sampling should start at the next time it is synchronized to the full hour. Sampling is synchronized to the full hour if seconds since midnight modulo sample interval in seconds is equal to 0.

parameters.name: PreferredSampleInterval
parameters.description:
Preferred sample interval time. In order to get correlatable metrics, it is recommended that the sample interval be chosen in a way that 3600 modulo sample interval time in seconds is equal to 0. 
It is the responsibility of the CIM metric service implementation to decide whether the requested sample interval time is honored. 
The CIM client can check whether or not the metric providers are honoring the requested sample interval time by retrieving related BaseMetricDefinition instances and checking the contents of the "CIM_BaseMetricDefinition.SampleInterval" property.

parameters.name: RestartGathering
parameters.description: Boolean that when set to TRUE requests that gathering of all metrics associated to the metric service is re-started with this method call.

classes.name: Msvm_MigrationJob
classes.description: This class represents a migration operation job created for storage orvirtual system migration by virtual system migration service.

properties.name: Cancellable
properties.description: Indicates whether the job can be cancelled. The value of this property does not guarantee that a request to cancel the job will succeed.

properties.name: MigrationType
properties.description: The migration type as defined in Msvm_VirtualSystemMigrationSettingData.MigrationType. This can beused to identify the type of migration the job represents.

properties.name: VirtualSystemName
properties.description: GUID of the affected virtual system.

properties.name: DestinationHost
properties.description: Hostname the destination virtualization platform where the virtual system is migrating. It will be NULL for storage migration.

properties.name: NewSystemSettingData
properties.description: For Live Migration this will be set to NULL. For Storage Migration, it will indicate the new path for virtual machine data (ExternalDataRoot, SnapshotDataRoot and SwapFileDataRoot).  This being NULL indicates that virtual machine's data roots are not moving.  Otherwise NewSystemSettingData will be instance of Msvm_VirtualSystemGlobalSettingData containing properties ExternalDataRoot, SnapshotDataRoot and SwapFileDataRoot. If not NULL, ExternalDataRoot property will specify new location of virtual machine's current configuration and saved state. If not NULL, SnapshotDataRoot property will specify new location for snapshots data. If not NULL, SwapFileDataRoot property will specify new location of virtual machine's Smart Paging files

properties.name: NewResourceSettingData
properties.description: For Live Migration this must be set to NULL. For Storage Migration, it will specify the virtual machine's virtual hard drives which moved and where they moved. If not NULL, NewResourceSettingData will contain an array of embedded instances of resource allocation setting data (RASD) which represent VHDs. Destination location will be provided using Connection property of RASD.

properties.name: JobType

Values: Checking Virtual Machine and Storage Compatibility

enum.label: Checking Virtual Machine and Storage Compatibility

properties.description: Indicates the type of Job being tracked by this object.

properties.name: MigrationStateList

qualifiers.Description: Maintains an ordered list of attained migration states.

properties.description: Maintains an ordered list of attained migration states.

parameters.name: RequestedState
parameters.description:
RequestStateChange changes the state of a job. The possible values are as follows: 
Start (2) changes the state to 'Running'. 
Suspend (3) stops the job temporarily. The intention is to subsequently restart the job with 'Start'. It might be possible to enter the 'Service' state while suspended. (This is job-specific.) 
Terminate (4) stops the job cleanly, saving data, preserving the state, and shutting down all underlying processes in an orderly manner. 
Kill (5) terminates the job immediately with no requirement to save data or preserve the state. 
Service (6) puts the job into a vendor-specific service state. It might be possible to restart the job.

parameters.name: TimeoutPeriod
parameters.description:
A timeout period that specifies the maximum amount of time that the client expects the transition to the new state to take. The interval format must be used to specify the TimeoutPeriod. A value of 0 or a null parameter indicates that the client has no time requirements for the transition. 
If this property does not contain 0 or null and the implementation does not support this parameter, a return code of 'Use Of Timeout Parameter Not Supported' must be returned.

parameters.name: Error
parameters.description: If the OperationalStatus on the Job is not "OK", then this method will return a CIM Error instance. Otherwise, when the Job is "OK", null is returned.

methods.name: GetErrorEx
methods.description: When the job is executing or has terminated without error, then this method returns no Msvm_Error instance. However, if the job has failed because of some internal problem or because the job has been terminated by a client, then one or more Msvm_Error instances are returned.

classes.name: Msvm_MostCurrentSnapshotInBranch
classes.description:
The Msvm_MostCurrentSnapshotInBranch association associates an instance of the Msvm_ComputerSystem or Msvm_PlannedComputerSystem class representing a virtual system, and the instance of the Msvm_VirtualSystemSettingData class representing the virtual system snapshot that is the most current snapshot in a branch of dependent snapshots. 
Note that a new branch is implied as a snapshot that is part of an existing branch is applied to a virtual system, such that in this case initially that snapshot is the most current snapshot in that new branch. Note that this association does not exist if the virtual system was not activated based on a snapshot or no snapshot was yet created from the current instance of the virtual system.

properties.name: Antecedent
properties.description: Reference to the instance of the Msvm_ComputerSystem or Msvm_PlannedComputerSystem class representing a virtual system.

properties.name: Dependent
properties.description: Reference to the instance of the Msvm_VirtualSystemSettingData class representing the virtual system snapshot that is the most current snapshot in a branch of dependent snapshots.

properties.name: VirtualSystemIdentifiers
properties.description:
A free-form string array of identifiers of this resource presented to the virtual computer system's operating system. The indexes and values per index are defined on a per resource basis (that is, for each enumerated ResourceType value). This property is set to "GUID".
This is a read-only property, but it can be changed using the ModifyVirtualSystemResources method of the sd class.

properties.name: VirtualFunctions
properties.description: The virtual function number to assign to the VM.

properties.name: AllowDirectTranslatedP2P
properties.description: The property that defines if Direct Translated P2P is enabled or not.

properties.name: NumaAwarePlacement
properties.description: Specifies whether the set of NUMA nodes on which the virtual machine is allocated must include the node to which this device is affinitized.

properties.name: TargetVtl
properties.description: Reserved.

properties.name: AllowUntranslatedP2P
properties.description: The property that defines if Direct Untranslated P2P is enabled or not.

properties.name: GuestPciExpressMode
properties.description: Specifies the PCI fabric in the guest to which to attach this device.

classes.name: Msvm_PersistentMemoryController
classes.description: Represents a virtual persistent memory controller.

classes.name: Msvm_PersistentStorageSettingData
classes.description: Represents the persistent storage shared between a container host and a container.

properties.name: SourcePath
properties.description: The path to the resource to be shared.

properties.name: MappedLocation
properties.description: The location of the persistent storage within the container.

properties.name: ReadOnlyAccess
properties.description: Whether or not the container should have read-only access to the shared location (rather than read-write).

classes.name: Msvm_PhysicalGPUInfo
classes.description: Represents the physical GPU in the host.

classes.name: Msvm_PlannedComputerSystem
classes.description:
Represents a hosting computer system or virtual computer system.
To retrieve information for the VMMS, use Msvm_VirtualSystemManagementService.
Remarks: When a property of the Msvm_PlannedComputerSystem class changes, the WMI provider indicates an __InstanceModificationEvent event that describes the changes. The previous state is contained in the PreviousInstance property, and the new state is contained in the TargetInstance property. This event is asynchronous; by the time the __InstanceModificationEvent event is processed, the TargetInstance property may not reflect the current state.

properties.name: AssignedNumaNodeList
properties.description: An array of NUMA nodes that are currently assigned to the virtual machine.

properties.name: OnTimeInMilliseconds
properties.description: For the virtual system, this property describes the total up time, in milliseconds, since the machine was last turned on, reset, or restored. This time excludes the time the virtual system was in the paused state. For the host system, this property is set to NULL.

properties.name: ProcessID
properties.description: The identifier of the process under which this virtual machine is running. This value can be used to uniquely identify the instance of Vmwp.exe on the system that is running the virtual machine.

properties.name: TimeOfLastConfigurationChange
properties.description: The date and time when the virtual machine configuration file was last modified. The configuration file is modified during certain virtual machine operations, as well as when any of the virtual machine or device settings are added, modified, or removed.

parameters.name: RequestedState
parameters.description: The state requested for the element. This information will be placed into the RequestedState property of the instance if the return code of the RequestStateChange method is 0 ('Completed with No Error'), or 4096 (0x1000) ('Job Started'). Refer to the description of the EnabledState and RequestedState properties for the detailed explanations of the RequestedState values.

parameters.name: Job
parameters.description: May contain a reference to the ConcreteJob created to track the state transition initiated by the method invocation.

parameters.name: TimeoutPeriod
parameters.description:
A timeout period that specifies the maximum amount of time that the client expects the transition to the new state to take. The interval format must be used to specify the TimeoutPeriod. A value of 0 or a null parameter indicates that the client has no time requirements for the transition. 
If this property does not contain 0 or null and the implementation does not support this parameter, a return code of 'Use Of Timeout Parameter Not Supported' shall be returned.

classes.name: Msvm_Processor
classes.description: Represents the virtual processor in a virtual computer system.

properties.name: LoadPercentageHistory
properties.description: The recorded history of percentage of the total processing power being consumed by the virtual system. This is an array containing samples.

classes.name: Msvm_ProcessorPool
classes.description: Aggregates the processor resources that may be allocated to a virtual system.

methods.name: CalculatePossibleReserve
methods.description:
This method is used to find the actual reserve with the input parameter being the number of virtual machine processors for which the reserve is calculated. This method is necessary because the reservation of processor resources is highly dependent on the number of processors which must be scheduled in parallel.
Windows Server 2008:  Before Windows Server 2008 R2 this method does nothing and always returns 0./nReturn value: The amount of CPU resources that may be reserved for a virtual system.

classes.name: Msvm_ProcessorSettingData
classes.description: Aggregates the processor resources that may be allocated to a virtual system.

properties.name: LimitCPUID
properties.description: Indicates whether the virtual machine should lower the CPU identifier. Some older operating systems may require you to limit processor functionality in this way in order to run.

properties.name: LimitProcessorFeatures
properties.description: Indicates whether the VM should limit the CPU features exposed to the operating system. Limiting the processor features enables the VM to be migrated to different host computer systems with different processors. Migrating VMs between computers with processors from different vendors is not supported.

properties.name: MaxProcessorsPerNumaNode
properties.description: The maximum number of virtual processors that can be observed within the VM as belonging to a single virtual NUMA node.

properties.name: MaxNumaNodesPerSocket
properties.description: The maximum number of NUMA nodes that can be observed within the VM as belonging to a single processor socket.

properties.name: EnableHostResourceProtection
properties.description: Indicates whether the VM should enable features that increase the protection of host  resources from workload running in the VM.

properties.name: HwThreadsPerCore
properties.description: Indicates the number of SMT threads per core reported to the guest.  This reporting is independent of whether the hardware for SMT is present.

properties.name: ExposeVirtualizationExtensions
properties.description: Indicates whether Hyper-V should expose virtualized hardware virtualization extensionsto the VM.

properties.name: HideHypervisorPresent
properties.description: Indicates whether Hyper-V should report that a hypervisor is present to the nested guest

properties.name: CpuGroupId
properties.description: The Cpu Group Id this VM is bound to. When value is 0 it means is not bound to a specificcpu group.

properties.name: DisableSpeculationControls
properties.description: Indicates whether the protections for speculative execution should be disabled for the virtual processors.

properties.name: EnablePerfmonPmu
properties.description: Indicates whether Hyper-V should expose virtualized PMU (Performance Monitoring Unit) facilities to the VM.

properties.name: EnablePerfmonLbr
properties.description: Indicates whether Hyper-V should expose virtualized LBR (Last Branch Record) facilities to the VM.

properties.name: EnablePerfmonPebs
properties.description: Indicates whether Hyper-V should expose virtualized PEBS (Processor Event-Based Sampling) facilities to the VM.

properties.name: EnablePerfmonIpt
properties.description: Indicates whether Hyper-V should expose virtualized IPT (Intel Processor Trace) facilities to the VM.

properties.name: EnablePageShattering
properties.description: Indicates whether Hyper-V should enable page shattering for the VM

properties.name: EnableLegacyApicMode
properties.description: Deprecated.

properties.name: AllowACountMCount
properties.description: Indicates whether Hyper-V should allow the VM to access ACOUNT/MCOUNT MSRs for CPU performance monitoring.

properties.name: LimitProcessorFeaturesMode
properties.description: Indicates the type of the CPU feature set that is applied when LimitProcessorFeaturesis enabled. DefaultMinimumFeatures applies a fixed minimum feature set to the VM,ClusterNodeCommonFeatures applies a dynamically calculated feature set based on thecapabilities of the nodes in a Failover Cluster.

properties.name: ApicMode
properties.description: Indicates the APIC mode for the VM

properties.name: CpuBrandString
properties.description: Provides a custom CPU brand string to expose to the VM. If this field is notset, the VM will see the original hardware value at boot.

properties.name: PerfCpuFreqCapMhz
properties.description: Provides the maximum CPU frequency for a VM. This field may not be obeyed depending on host capabilities.

properties.name: MaxProcessorCountPerL3
properties.description: Maximum number of processors sharing L3 cache. This field cannot be adjusted after VM start.

properties.name: MaxClusterCountPerSocket
properties.description: Maximum number of clusters in a socket. This field cannot be adjusted after VM start.

properties.name: ProcessorFeatureSet
properties.description: Indicates the CPU feature set exposed to the operating system.

properties.name: L3CacheWays
properties.description: Provides the number of L3 cache ways to report for a VM. This field cannot be adjusted after VM start, and may not be obeyed depending on host capabilities.

properties.name: EnableSocketTopology
properties.description: Enable socket topology mapping when placing a VM on the host.

properties.name: ExtendedVirtualizationExtensions
properties.description: Specifies the set of additional virtualization extensions that should be exposed to the VM.

properties.name: MaxHwIsolatedGuests
properties.description: Maximum number of hardware isolated guests that the VM may run simulatenously.

properties.name: EnablePerfmonArchPmu
properties.description: Indicates whether Hyper-V should expose virtualized architectural PMU (Performance Monitoring Unit) facilities to the VM.

properties.name: L3ProcessorDistributionPolicy
properties.description: Indicate how Hyper-V distributes processors at the L3 level.This field cannot be adjusted after VM start.

properties.name: EnlightenmentSet
properties.description: Indicates the Enlightenment set exposed to the operating system.

properties.name: EnableHierarchicalVirtualization
properties.description: Indicates whether Hyper-V should enable hierarchical virtualization for the VM.

properties.name: MaxHierarchicalPartitions
properties.description: Maximum number of child partitions that a hierarchical virtualization partition can create.

properties.name: MaxHierarchicalVps
properties.description: Maximum number of child vps that a hierarchical virtualization partition can create.

properties.name: PartitionDiagnosticBufferCount
properties.description: The number of hypervisor tracing buffers that a hierarchical virtualization partition uses.

properties.name: PartitionDiagnosticBufferSizeInPages
properties.description: The size, in pages, of each hypervisor tracing buffer that a hierarchical virtualization partition uses.

properties.name: PhysicalAddressWidth
properties.description: The physical address width for the VM.

properties.name: EnableLayeredVirtualization
properties.description: Indicates whether Hyper-V should enable layered virtualization for the VM.

properties.name: MaximumHierarchicalVirtualProcessors
properties.description: Maximum number of child vps that a hierarchical virtualization partition can create.

properties.name: MaximumLayeredVirtualProcessors
properties.description: Maximum number of child vps that a layered virtualization partition can create.

properties.name: PerfCpuFreqMinMhz
properties.description: Provides the minimum CPU frequency for a VM. This field may not be obeyed depending on host capabilities.

properties.name: PerfCpuFreqDesiredMhz
properties.description: Provides the desired CPU frequency for a VM. This field may not be obeyed depending on host capabilities.

properties.name: PerfCpuAutonomousActivityWindow
properties.description: Provides the CPU's autonomous activity window size for a VM. This field may not be obeyed depending on host capabilities. This field is only effective when PerfCpuFreqDesiredMhz is set to 0.

properties.name: PerfCpuEnergyPerformancePreference
properties.description: Provides the CPU's Energy Performance Preference value for a VM. This field may not be obeyed depending on host capabilities. This field is only effective when PerfCpuFreqDesiredMhz is set to 0.

properties.name: PerfCpuIgnoreHostMaxFrequency
properties.description: Controls whether host OS policy should limit the maximum CPU frequency for a VM. When true, PerfCpuFreqCapMhz can be set above the host policy limit, up to the maximum advertised by the CPU.

properties.name: VmmCapabilitySet
properties.description: Indicates the VMM capability set exposed to the operating system.

properties.name: VmmAssignableSyntheticPF
properties.description: Indicates the Vmm Assignable Synthetic PF exposed to the operating system.

properties.name: LpiMode
properties.description: Indicates whether LPI (Locality-specific Peripheral Interrupts) is enabled for this virtual machine.

classes.name: Msvm_ProtocolControllerForUnit
classes.description: This association indicates a subclass of logical device (for example a storage volume) is connected through a specific protocol controller. In many situations (for example storage LUN masking), there may be many of these associations used to relate to different objects. Therefore, subclasses have been defined to optimize enumeration of the associations.

classes.name: Msvm_Ps2Mouse
classes.description: Represents a PS2 mouse device. Instances of this class are logical devices that are always present in a virtual system, and thus are not allocated through a resource pool. One instance is always present in a virtual computer system.

properties.name: AbsoluteCoordinates
properties.description: Indicates whether the device operates on absolute coordinates. If not set, the device's coordinates are relative. This property is set to FALSE.

methods.name: GetButtonState
methods.description:
Retrieves the current state of the specified device button.
Return value: A return value of zero indicates success. A non-zero value indicates a query failure.

methods.name: SetButtonState
methods.description:
Sets the current state of the specified device button.
Return value: A return value of zero indicates success. A non-zero value indicates a failure to modify the button state.

methods.name: ClickButton
methods.description:
Simulates a button click, a down-up sequence, on the specified device button.
Return value: A return value of zero indicates success. A non-zero value indicates a failure to modify the button state.

methods.name: SetRelativePosition
methods.description:
Offsets the position of the mouse by the specified horizontal and vertical deltas.
Return value: A return value of zero indicates success. A non-zero value indicates a failure to alter the mouse position.

methods.name: SetScrollPosition
methods.description:
Adjusts the z-coordinate of the wheel control of the pointing device. Values written to this property are always relative coordinate offsets.
Return value: A return value of zero indicates success. A non-zero value indicates a failure to alter the scroll position.

classes.name: Msvm_RdvComponent
classes.description: Represents the state of the RDV component, which is responsible for providing a transport for parent to the guest for configuration purposes.

methods.name: EnableEndPoints
methods.description:
Requests the Remote Desktop Services Virtual Device to Start a Pipe connection with the virtual machine.
If zero (0) is returned, then the operation was successful. Any other return code indicates an error condition.

classes.name: Msvm_RdvComponentSettingData
classes.description:
Represents the configured state of the Remote Desktop Virtualization (RDV) component.
The default state is Enabled.

properties.name: EnabledState
properties.description:
The enabled and disabled states of an element.
Valid values are 2 (enabled) and 3 (disabled). The default value is 2 (enabled).
This is a read-only property, but it can be changed using the ModifyVirtualSystemResources method of the Msvm_VirtualSystemManagementService class.

classes.name: Msvm_ReferencePointCollection
classes.description: Represents a collection of virtual system reference points.

properties.name: CollectionID
properties.description: The unique identification of the Collection object.

properties.name: ElementName
properties.description: An user-defined name for the collection. Note this is not guaranteed to be unique.

properties.name: ReferencePointType
properties.description:
Indicates the type of the reference point. 
Log based: Hyper-V replica log tracking. 
RCT based: Based on Resilient Change Tracking of virtual disks.

properties.name: ConsistencyLevel
properties.description:
Consistency level of the reference point.
Crash Consistent: The reference point indicates a point in time when the virtual system was in crash consistent state. 
Application Consistent: The reference point indicates a point in time when the virtual system was in application consistent state.

properties.name: VirtualSystemCollectionId
properties.description: The identifier of the Msvm_VirtualSystemCollection to which this reference point belongs

properties.name: HasAssociatedLog
properties.description: This property is set to TRUE if all the member reference points have associated logs. This is valid only for log based reference points. For RCT based reference points this is set to FALSE. For log based reference points, once the log is discarded this value becomes FALSE.

classes.name: Msvm_ReferencePointOfVirtualSystem
classes.description: Associates the Msvm_VirtualSystemReferencePoint to the corresponding Msvm_VirtualSystem objects.

properties.name: AllowedPrimaryHostSystem
properties.description: The primary server that this entry represents.

properties.name: TrustGroup

properties.name: ReplicaStorageLocation

classes.name: Msvm_ReplicationProvider
classes.description: Replication provider available for replication of virtual system to specified endpoint.

properties.name: Name
properties.description:
Globally unique identifier of provider that identifies the endpoint provider. For host to host inbuilt this Name is fixed as 22391CDC-272C-4DDF-BA88-9BEFB1A0975C.
In case of external provider this is CLSID of provider COM class object.

classes.name: Msvm_ReplicationRelationship
classes.description: Represents the replication relationship for a computer system.

properties.name: InstanceID

qualifiers.Description:
InstanceID is an optional property that may be used to opaquely and uniquely identify an instance of this class within the scope of the instantiating Namespace. Various subclasses of this class may override this property to make it required, or a key. Such subclasses may also modify the preferred algorithms for ensuring uniqueness that are defined below.
To ensure uniqueness within the NameSpace, the value of InstanceID should be constructed using the following "preferred" algorithm: 
<OrgID>:<LocalID> 
Where <OrgID> and <LocalID> are separated by a colon (:), and where <OrgID> must include a copyrighted, trademarked, or otherwise unique name that is owned by the business entity that is creating or defining the InstanceID or that is a registered ID assigned to the business entity by a recognized global authority. (This requirement is similar to the <Schema Name>_<Class Name> structure of Schema class names.) In addition, to ensure uniqueness, <OrgID> must not contain a colon (:). When using this algorithm, the first colon to appear in InstanceID must appear between <OrgID> and <LocalID>. 
<LocalID> is chosen by the business entity and should not be reused to identify different underlying (real-world) elements. If not null and the above "preferred" algorithm is not used, the defining entity must assure that the resulting InstanceID is not reused across any InstanceIDs produced by this or other providers for the NameSpace of this instance. 
If not set to null for DMTF-defined instances, the "preferred" algorithm must be used with the <OrgID> set to CIM.

properties.name: ReplicationState
properties.description: Replication state for the virtual machine.

properties.name: ReplicationHealth
properties.description: Replication health for the virtual machine.

properties.name: FailedOverReplicationType
properties.description: Type of failover that was performed for the virtual machine.

properties.name: LastReplicationType
properties.description: Type of the last replication that was received for the virtual machine.

properties.name: LastApplicationConsistentReplicationTime
properties.description: The time at which the last application consistent replication is received on recovery for the virtual machine.

properties.name: LastReplicationTime
properties.description: The time at which the last replication is received on recovery for the virtual machine.

properties.name: LastApplyTime
properties.description: The time at which the last replication is applied on recovery for the virtual machine.

classes.name: Msvm_ReplicationService
classes.description: Manages the failover replication for a virtual machine.

methods.name: ModifyServiceSettings
methods.description: Modifies the setting data for the Hyper-V Replica service.

methods.name: AddAuthorizationEntry
methods.description: This method add an authorization entry to the server. This entry is used for authorizing the connections to the recovery server.

methods.name: ModifyAuthorizationEntry
methods.description: This method modifies an authorization entry in the server.

methods.name: RemoveAuthorizationEntry
methods.description: This method removes an authorization entry from the server.

methods.name: TestReplicationConnection
methods.description: Verifies if the replication can be enabled from the currenthost system to the specified recovery system.

methods.name: CreateReplicationRelationship
methods.description: Creates new replication relationship for virtual machine.

methods.name: ModifyReplicationSettings
methods.description: Modifies the setting data for the virtual machine replication relationship.

methods.name: RemoveReplicationRelationship
methods.description:
Removes the virtual machine replication relationship.
This does not clean up the recovery server virtual machine.

methods.name: StartReplication
methods.description:
Starts the virtual machine replication.
Specify InitialReplicationType to determine the type of transfer to be used for initial replication.

methods.name: ImportInitialReplica
methods.description:
Imports the virtual machine initial replication.
Specify InitialReplicationImportLocation where primary server exported the replication.

methods.name: ReverseReplicationRelationship
methods.description: On recovery using this API a failed over VM can be replicated back to primary server

methods.name: InitiateFailover
methods.description: Sets the failover for virtual machine to application or crash consistent image.

methods.name: RevertFailover
methods.description:
Undo the current failover for the virtual machine. After this operation InitiateFailover can be called again.
This operation will work only for the virtual machine which  is in failedover state after using PreapreFailover.

methods.name: CommitFailover
methods.description: Commits the recovery snapshot that InitiateFailover has used for failover.

methods.name: TestReplicaSystem
methods.description:
This API created the test system for the replicated virtual machine.
User can select the snapshot from which the test system should be created. Every successful TestReplicaSystem needs to be followed by DestroySystem.
Relationship between parent system and test system can be derived from association class Msvm_AssociatedTestFailoverSystem.

parameters.name: RequestedState
parameters.description: The state requested for the element. This information will be placed into the RequestedState property of the instance if the return code of the RequestStateChange method is 0 ('Completed with No Error'), or 4096 (0x1000) ('Job Started'). Refer to the description of the EnabledState and RequestedState properties for the detailed explanations of the RequestedState values.

parameters.name: Job
parameters.description: May contain a reference to the ConcreteJob created to track the state transition initiated by the method invocation.

parameters.name: TimeoutPeriod
parameters.description:
A timeout period that specifies the maximum amount of time that the client expects the transition to the new state to take. The interval format must be used to specify the TimeoutPeriod. A value of 0 or a null parameter indicates that the client has no time requirements for the transition. 
If this property does not contain 0 or null and the implementation does not support this parameter, a return code of 'Use Of Timeout Parameter Not Supported' shall be returned.

methods.name: Resynchronize
methods.description: This method performs Resynchronize operation on the given Virtual Machine.

methods.name: SetFailoverNetworkAdapterSettings
methods.description: Configures the network adapters within the guest operating system. These configuration parameters are applied after a failover operation, immediately upon establishing communication with the KVP Exchange integration component running within the guest operating system.

methods.name: GetReplicationStatistics
methods.description: Retrieves replication statistics associated with virtual machine.

methods.name: ResetReplicationStatistics
methods.description: Resets replication statistics associated with virtual machine.

methods.name: SetAuthorizationEntry
methods.description: This method associates the authorization setting data for virtual machine

methods.name: GetSystemCertificates
methods.description: This method returns the system certificates on a host system.

methods.name: RemoveReplicationRelationshipEx
methods.description:
Removes the virtual machine specified replication relationship.
This does not clean up the recovery server virtual machine.

methods.name: GetReplicationStatisticsEx
methods.description: Retrieves replication statistics associated with specified replication relationship of virtual machine.

methods.name: ResetReplicationStatisticsEx
methods.description: Resets replication statistics associated with specified replication relationship of virtual machine.

methods.name: ChangeReplicationModeToPrimary
methods.description: Using this API, a replica VM having extended replication relationship can become new primary and continue its replication with the extended replica VM. This API requires the replica VM to be in a failover committed state.

methods.name: InitiateFailback
methods.description: Sets the failover for virtual machine to application or crash consistent image.

classes.name: Msvm_ReplicationServiceSettingData
classes.description:
Represents the settings for the failover replication service on a recovery host.
The properties for this class cannot be modified directly. The client must call the Msvm_ReplicationService.ModifyServiceSettings method to modify any of these properties.

properties.name: RecoveryServerEnabled
properties.description: Enable hyper-v host as recovery server.

properties.name: AllowedAuthenticationType

Values: Certificate based authentication and kerberos authentication

enum.label: Certificate based authentication and kerberos authentication

properties.description: Define authentication modes allowed for connecting to recovery host server.

properties.name: CertificateThumbPrint
properties.description: Certificate thumbprint to use when AuthenticationType is certificate based authentication.

properties.name: HttpsPort
properties.description: Recovery server port number to use when accepting secure connection for replication.

properties.name: HttpPort
properties.description: Recovery server port number to use when accepting unsecure connection for replication.

properties.name: MonitoringStartTime
properties.description: Start time for replica monitoring. By default monitoring starts at 10:00am UTC. Specify the datetime using time elements only.

properties.name: MonitoringInterval
properties.description: Interval in seconds at which replica statistics should be reset. By default this is 24 hrs. Valid range is 4 hrs to 7 days. Valid values are aligned to 24 hrs cycle. Valid values are 4 hrs, 6 hrs, 8 hrs, 12 hrs, 1 day to 7 days.

classes.name: Msvm_ReplicationSettingData
classes.description: Failover Replication Setting for virtual machine.

properties.name: AuthenticationType
properties.description: Define authentication mode used to connect to recovery server.

properties.name: CertificateThumbPrint
properties.description: Certificate thumbprint to use when AuthenticationType is certificate based authorization.

properties.name: RootCertificateThumbPrint
properties.description: Root Certificate thumbprint of the certificate in use  when AuthenticationType is certificate based authorization.

properties.name: CompressionEnabled
properties.description: Enable compression of replication data while sending it to recovery server.

properties.name: BypassProxyServer
properties.description: Bypass proxy server while connectng it to recovery server.

properties.name: RecoveryConnectionPoint
properties.description: Name of connection point, in case of recovery cluster this is the broker CAP name and in case of standalone recovery server this is the host system name. In case of external provider, this represents the connection point specific to the provider.

properties.name: RecoveryHostSystem
properties.description: FQDN of recovery host system which is hosting the virtual machine.

properties.name: PrimaryConnectionPoint
properties.description: Name of connection point, in case of primary cluster this is the broker CAP name and in case of standalone primary server this is the host system name.

properties.name: PrimaryHostSystem
properties.description: FQDN of primary host system which is hosting the virtual machine.

properties.name: RecoveryServerPortNumber
properties.description: Recovery server port number to use when making secure connection for replication.

properties.name: ReplicateHostKvpItems
properties.description: Enable or disable of host-only Msvm_KvpExchangeDataItem from primary virtual machine to recovery virtual machine. Default value is TRUE.

properties.name: ApplicationConsistentSnapshotInterval
properties.description: Time interval between application consistent snapshots, specified in unit of hours. Valid range is from 1 hour to 12 hours in step of 1 hour.

properties.name: RecoveryHistory
properties.description:
Maximum number of recovery snapshots stored at the recovery server.
Valid range is from 0 to 16 for the Hyper-V to Hyper-V replication. In case of external provider, range is as supported by the endpoint provider.

properties.name: IncludedDisks

qualifiers.Description: The list of VHD attached to the ComputerSystem that will be replicated by the Failover Replication Engine. This is an array of strings each containing an the InstanceID of the resource allocation setting data (RASD) of the VHD.

properties.description: The list of VHD attached to the ComputerSystem that will be replicated by the Failover Replication Engine. This is an array of strings each containing an the InstanceID of the resource allocation setting data (RASD) of the VHD.

properties.name: AutoResynchronizeEnabled
properties.description: Automatically trigger resynchronize operation on replication errors due to power and hardware failures. Default value is TRUE.

properties.name: AutoResynchronizeIntervalStart
properties.description:
Automatically trigger resynchronize operation on replication errors due to power and hardware failures when they occur after the time specified by this parameter and AutoResynchronizeEnabled is TRUE.
Specify the datetime using time elements only. Default value is 1830 hours local time.

properties.name: AutoResynchronizeIntervalEnd
properties.description:
Automatically trigger resynchronize operation on replication errors due to power and hardware failures when they occur before the time specified by this parameter and AutoResynchronizeEnabled is TRUE.
Specify the datetime using time elements only. Default value is 0600 hours local time.

properties.name: EnableWriteOrderPreservationAcrossDisks
properties.description: Enabling this property ensures all replicating virtual hard disks for the virtual machine are replicated to the same point in time. This ensures replication honors the write-order of the applications in the virtual machine.

properties.name: ReplicationProvider
properties.description: Path to instance of class Msvm_ReplicationProvider that identifies the replication provider endpoint.

properties.name: ReplicationInterval
properties.description:
Replication Interval of a replication relationship in seconds.
Valid values are 30, 300, 900.

properties.name: AdditionalSettings
properties.description: Additional replication settings which can be used by endpoint provider.

classes.name: Msvm_ReplicationStatistics
classes.description: Provides replication statistics for a virtual machine

properties.name: StartStatisticTime
properties.description: The time when the Hyper-V Replica service started gathering replication statistics data.

properties.name: EndStatisticTime
properties.description: The time when the Hyper-V Replica service stopped gathering replication statistics data.At this time new replication statistics cycle starts.

properties.name: ReplicationSuccessCount
properties.description: Total successful replication count.

properties.name: ReplicationSize
properties.description: Total replication data transferred to the recovery host system.

properties.name: MaxReplicationSize
properties.description: Maximum replication data transferred to the recovery host system.

properties.name: ReplicationLatency
properties.description: Total replication latency while transferring the data to the recovery host system.

properties.name: ReplicationMissCount
properties.description: Represents missed replication cycles. This can be because of heavy workload, network issues or replication errors.

properties.name: MaxReplicationLatency
properties.description: Maximum time taken to complete a single replication cycle.

properties.name: NetworkFailureCount
properties.description: Total network error count for replication channel with recovery host system.

properties.name: ReplicationFailureCount
properties.description: Replication error count. This includes errors for replication operation.

properties.name: PendingReplicationSize
properties.description: Pending replication data size.

properties.name: ApplicationConsistentSnapshotFailureCount
properties.description: Total error count for VSS snapshot failures.

properties.name: ReplicationHealth
properties.description: Represents the current replication.

properties.name: LastTestFailoverTime
properties.description: Indicates the last time test replica system was created for a replication enabled virtual system on recovery server.

classes.name: Msvm_ResourceAllocationFromPool
classes.description: Associates an instance of a resource allocation with the resource pool from which it is allocated.

classes.name: Msvm_ResourceAllocationSettingData
classes.description: Represents the current and recorded allocation states of a virtual resource.

properties.name: VirtualSystemIdentifiers
properties.description:
A free-form string array of identifiers of this resource presented to the virtual computer system's operating system. These values are only used if the ResourceType property is set to 6 (Parallel SCSI HBA) and the ResourceSubType property is set to "Microsoft Synthetic SCSI Controller". This property is set to "GUID".
This is a read-only property, but it can be changed using the ModifyVirtualSystemResources method of the Msvm_VirtualSystemManagementService class.
Windows Server 2008:  The VirtualSystemIdentifiers property is not supported until Windows Server 2008 R2.

properties.name: TargetVtl
properties.description: Reserved.

classes.name: Msvm_ResourceDependentOnResource
classes.description: This association establishes that a CIM_ResourceAllocationSettingData instance representing a resource allocation depends on another resource allocation.

properties.name: Antecedent

qualifiers.Description: Antecedent represents the independent resource in this association.

properties.description: Antecedent represents the independent resource in this association.

properties.name: Dependent

qualifiers.Description: Dependent represents the resource that is dependent on the Antecedent.

properties.description: Dependent represents the resource that is dependent on the Antecedent.

classes.name: Msvm_ResourcePool
classes.description: A ResourcePool is a logical entity (with associated controls) provided by the host system for the purpose of allocation and assignment of resources. A given ResourcePool may be used to allocate resources of a specific type. Hierarchies of ResourcePools may be created to provide administrative control over allocations.

classes.name: Msvm_ResourcePoolComponent
classes.description: Represents a resource pool component of the Microsoft Windows Hyper-V platform.

properties.name: AllocationCapabilitiesClassName
properties.description: The name of the class derived from CIM_AllocationCapabilities that describes the allocation capabilities of this resource pool.

properties.name: ResourcePoolClassName
properties.description: The name of the class derived from CIM_ResourcePool that implements the resource pool.

properties.name: ResourcePoolSettingDataClassName
properties.description: The name of the class derived from CIM_SettingData that describes the non-allocation related settings of the resource pool.

properties.name: PhysicalDeviceClassName
properties.description: The name of the class derived from CIM_LogicalDevice the implements the physical device from which this pool allocates resources. This property may be NULL if the virtual device class allocated from the this pool is the same as the physical device class.

properties.name: WmiFactoryCLSID
properties.description: A GUID that represents the class indentifier of the component's WMI object factory.

properties.name: MaxParentPools
properties.description: The maximum number of parent resource pools that a child pool supports.

classes.name: Msvm_ResourcePoolConfigurationCapabilities

qualifiers.Description: Msvm_ResourcePoolConfigurationCapabilities describes the capabilities of the associated Msvm_ResourcePoolConfigurationService. Clients may use instances of this class to determine which methods are supported. The same method must not be listed in both lists. Implementations must either be non-blocking and use a Job for long running operations, or always block until the operation completes.

classes.description: Msvm_ResourcePoolConfigurationCapabilities describes the capabilities of the associated Msvm_ResourcePoolConfigurationService. Clients may use instances of this class to determine which methods are supported. The same method must not be listed in both lists. Implementations must either be non-blocking and use a Job for long running operations, or always block until the operation completes.

properties.name: AsynchronousMethodsSupported

qualifiers.Description: This property reflects the methods of the associated service class that are supported that may return a Job.

properties.description: This property reflects the methods of the associated service class that are supported that may return a Job.

properties.name: SynchronousMethodsSupported

qualifiers.Description: This property reflects the methods of the associated service class that are supported and block until completed (no Job is returned.)

properties.description: This property reflects the methods of the associated service class that are supported and block until completed (no Job is returned.)

classes.name: Msvm_ResourcePoolConfigurationService

qualifiers.Description: This service provides for active management of ResourcePools. It allows jobs to be started for the creation modification and deletion of ResourcePools as well as the modification of the ResourcePool ResourceSettings data.

classes.description: This service provides for active management of ResourcePools. It allows jobs to be started for the creation modification and deletion of ResourcePools as well as the modification of the ResourcePool ResourceSettings data.

methods.name: CreatePool

qualifiers.Description: Starts a job to create a child ResourcePool. The ResourcePool will be scoped to the same System as this Service. If 0 is returned, then the task completed successfully and the use of ConcreteJob was not required. If the task will take some time to complete, a ConcreteJob will be created and its reference returned in the output parameter Job. The resulting pool will be a child pool.
Values: Incorrect Resource Type for the Pool

return_codes.label: Incorrect Resource Type for the Pool

methods.description: Starts a job to create a child ResourcePool. The ResourcePool will be scoped to the same System as this Service. If 0 is returned, then the task completed successfully and the use of ConcreteJob was not required. If the task will take some time to complete, a ConcreteJob will be created and its reference returned in the output parameter Job. The resulting pool will be a child pool.

parameters.name: PoolSettings
parameters.description: String containing an embedded instance of a Msvm_ResourcePoolSettingData instance that is used to specify the pools non-allocation related settings.

parameters.name: ParentPools
parameters.description: The Pool(s) from which to create the new Pool.

parameters.name: AllocationSettings
parameters.description: String containing one or more embedded instances of CIM_ResourceAllocationSettingData that is used to specify the pools allocation related settings. This array must contain either one element for each elemnt in the ParentPools array or exactly one element. If this array contains one element and ParentPools contains more than one element, the AlllocationSettings specifies a shared capacity allocation that can be satisfied by any of the parent pools. This is used to restrict the resources that can be allocated from the child to pool to a lower limit than the aggregate capacity provided by its parents. This option is not supported by all resource types. If a resource type does not support shared capacity allocation, this method shall return "Not Supported".

parameters.name: Pool
parameters.description: A reference to the resulting pool.

parameters.name: Job
parameters.description: Reference to the job (may be null if job completed).

methods.name: ModifyPoolResources

qualifiers.Description: Start a job to change parent pool resource settings for resources assigned to a child pool. If 0 is returned, the function completed successfully and no ConcreteJob instance was required. If 4096/0x1000 is returned, a ConcreteJob will be started to change the parent pool. The Job's reference will be returned in the output parameter Job.
Values: Incorrect Resource Type for the Pool

return_codes.label: Incorrect Resource Type for the Pool

methods.description: Start a job to change parent pool resource settings for resources assigned to a child pool. If 0 is returned, the function completed successfully and no ConcreteJob instance was required. If 4096/0x1000 is returned, a ConcreteJob will be started to change the parent pool. The Job's reference will be returned in the output parameter Job.

parameters.name: ChildPool
parameters.description: Reference to the child pool.

parameters.name: ParentPools
parameters.description: Reference to the parent pool(s).

parameters.name: AllocationSettings
parameters.description: Optional string containing a representation of a CIM_SettingData instance that is used to specify the settings for the Parent Pool.

parameters.name: Job
parameters.description: Reference to the job (may be null if job completed).

methods.name: ModifyPoolSettings

qualifiers.Description: Start a job to change the non-allocation related settings of a child. If 0 is returned, the function completed successfully and no ConcreteJob instance was required. If 4096/0x1000 is returned, a ConcreteJob will be started to change the settings. The Job's reference will be returned in the output parameter Job.
Values: Incorrect Resource Type for the Pool

return_codes.label: Incorrect Resource Type for the Pool

methods.description: Start a job to change the non-allocation related settings of a child. If 0 is returned, the function completed successfully and no ConcreteJob instance was required. If 4096/0x1000 is returned, a ConcreteJob will be started to change the settings. The Job's reference will be returned in the output parameter Job.

parameters.name: ChildPool
parameters.description: Reference to the child pool.

parameters.name: PoolSettings
parameters.description: String containing an embedded instance of a Msvm_ResourcePoolSettingData that is used to specify the settings for the Pool.

parameters.name: Job
parameters.description: Reference to the job (may be null if job completed).

methods.name: DeletePool

qualifiers.Description: Start a job to delete a ResourcePool. No allocations may be outstanding or the delete will fail with "In Use." If the resource pool is a root resource pool, any host resources are returned back to the underlying system. If 0 is returned, the function completed successfully, and no ConcreteJob was required. If 4096/0x1000 is returned, a ConcreteJob will be started to delete the ResourcePool. A reference to the Job is returned in the Job parameter.
Values: Incorrect Resource Type for the Pool

return_codes.label: Incorrect Resource Type for the Pool

methods.description: Start a job to delete a ResourcePool. No allocations may be outstanding or the delete will fail with "In Use." If the resource pool is a root resource pool, any host resources are returned back to the underlying system. If 0 is returned, the function completed successfully, and no ConcreteJob was required. If 4096/0x1000 is returned, a ConcreteJob will be started to delete the ResourcePool. A reference to the Job is returned in the Job parameter.

parameters.name: Pool
parameters.description: Reference to the pool to delete.

parameters.name: Job
parameters.description: Reference to the job (may be null if job completed).

classes.name: Msvm_ResourcePoolRegistration

properties.name: LogicalDeviceClassName

properties.name: OtherResourceType

properties.name: ResourceSubType
properties.description: A string that describes an implementation specific sub-type for this resource. For example, this may be used to distinguish different models of the same resource type. There is a correspondence with the ResourceSubType property of CIM_ResourceAllocationSettingData.

properties.name: ResourceType
properties.description: The type of resource this allocation setting represents. There is a correspondence with the ResourceType property of CIM_ResourceAllocationSettingData.

properties.name: SettingDataClassName
properties.description: The name of the class derived from CIM_ResourceAllocationSettingData that implements the settings for this resource type.

classes.name: Msvm_SecurityElement
classes.description: Represents the runtime security settings of a CIM_ComputerSystem.

properties.name: SystemCreationClassName

qualifiers.Description: The scoping System's CreationClassName.

properties.description: The scoping System's CreationClassName.

properties.name: SystemName

qualifiers.Description: The scoping System's Name.

properties.description: The scoping System's Name.

properties.name: CreationClassName

qualifiers.Description: CreationClassName indicates the name of the class or the subclass used in the creation of an instance. When used with the other key properties of this class, this property allows all instances of this class and its subclasses to be uniquely identified.

properties.description: CreationClassName indicates the name of the class or the subclass used in the creation of an instance. When used with the other key properties of this class, this property allows all instances of this class and its subclasses to be uniquely identified.

properties.name: Shielded
properties.description: Whether the VM is currently shielded.

properties.name: DataProtected
properties.description: Whether the VM is currently having its data protected.

properties.name: EncryptStateAndVmMigrationTrafficEnabled
properties.description: Whether the VM is currently having its state and migration traffic encrypted.

properties.name: MigTdHash
properties.description: Represents the migration policy that is known for the running isolated or confidential VM if non-empty. This property is read-only and returns an empty array if the host does not support TDX LM.

classes.name: Msvm_SecurityService
classes.description: Represents the security service. It is used for configuring virtual system security settings.

methods.name: ModifySecuritySettings
methods.description: Modifies the current security settings of a virtual machine.

parameters.name: SecuritySettingData
parameters.description: An embedded instance of class Msvm_SecuritySettingData that describes modifications to the current security settings of an existing virtual machine.

methods.name: SetKeyProtector
methods.description: Method to set the key protector for a virtual system.

methods.name: GetKeyProtector
methods.description: Method to retrieve the key protector for a virtual system.

methods.name: RestoreLastKnownGoodKeyProtector
methods.description: Method to restore back to the last known good key protector.

methods.name: SetSecurityPolicy
methods.description: Method to set the key protector for a virtual system.

parameters.name: AffectedSystem
parameters.description: Computer system (VM) which gets the new migration policy from the most recently set system-wide policy.

parameters.name: Job
parameters.description: An optional parameter for monitoring the progress of the operation when the operation is executing asynchronously. There is a distinctive status code for asynchronous start.

classes.name: Msvm_SecuritySettingData
classes.description:
Represents the configured state of the security settings for 
a virtual machine.

properties.name: TpmEnabled
properties.description:
A setting that indicates whether a Trusted Platform Module 
(TPM) should be enabled for this virtual machine. When this 
property is set to TRUE, then the TPM will be enabled. A  
newly created VM has a disabled TPM.

properties.name: ShieldingRequested
properties.description:
A setting that indicates whether shielding is requested for a VM.
This property is set to TRUE when a VM is to be shielded.
A newly created VM has an initial shielding requested state of FALSE.

properties.name: DataProtectionRequested
properties.description:
A setting that indicates whether data protection is requested for a VM.
This property is set to TRUE when a VM is to have its data protected.
A newly created VM has an initial data protection requested state of FALSE.

properties.name: KsdEnabled
properties.description:
A setting that indicates whether a Key Storage Drive 
(KSD) should be enabled for this virtual machine. When this 
property is set to TRUE, then the KSD will be enabled. A  
newly created VM has a disabled KSD.

properties.name: EncryptStateAndVmMigrationTraffic
properties.description:
A setting that indicates whether encryption of state and migration is enabled for a VM.
This property is set to TRUE when a VM is to have its state and migration traffic encrypted.
A newly created VM has an initial encryption of state and migration state of FALSE.

properties.name: VirtualizationBasedSecurityOptOut
properties.description:
A setting that indicates whether virtualization based security should be offered to a VM.
This property is set to TRUE when a VM should not be offered virtualization based security.
A newly created VM has an initial virtualization based security opt out state of FALSE.

properties.name: BindToHostTpm
properties.description:
A setting that indicates whether binding is requested for a VM.
This property is set to TRUE when a VM is to be bound to host.
A newly created VM has an initial bound state of FALSE.

properties.name: AppContainerLaunchOptOut
properties.description:
A setting that indicates whether the VM should launch under AppContainer (including LPAC).
This property is set to TRUE when a VM should NOT launch under AppContainer (it has opted out).
A newly created VM has an initial AppContainer opt-out value of FALSE.

properties.name: MigratablePolicy
properties.description:
A setting that controls migratability of the VM.
The value of 0 (Default) indicates that migration will be allowed by the policy.
For Intel TDX, the VM will use the host's configured MigTd hash.
The value of 1 (Disabled) indicates that LM is disabled for this VM.
Future values may be introduced as needed.

properties.name: TdxMrConfigId
properties.description: TDX measurement register configuration ID.

properties.name: TdxMrOwner
properties.description: TDX measurement register owner.

properties.name: TdxMrOwnerConfig
properties.description: TDX measurement register owner configuration.

classes.name: Msvm_SerialController
classes.description: Represents the capabilities and management of the serial controller. Only one serial controller can be active in a virtual machine at any time.  Serial controllers have a fixed number of port instances. This implementation supports two ports per controller.

parameters.name: RequestedState
parameters.description: The state requested for the element. This information will be placed into the RequestedState property of the instance if the return code of the RequestStateChange method is 0 ('Completed with No Error'), or 4096 (0x1000) ('Job Started'). Refer to the description of the EnabledState and RequestedState properties for the detailed explanations of the RequestedState values.

parameters.name: Job
parameters.description: May contain a reference to the ConcreteJob created to track the state transition initiated by the method invocation.

parameters.name: TimeoutPeriod
parameters.description:
A timeout period that specifies the maximum amount of time that the client expects the transition to the new state to take. The interval format must be used to specify the TimeoutPeriod. A value of 0 or a null parameter indicates that the client has no time requirements for the transition. 
If this property does not contain 0 or null and the implementation does not support this parameter, a return code of 'Use Of Timeout Parameter Not Supported' shall be returned.

classes.name: Msvm_SerialPort
classes.description: Represents a serial port associated with the serial controller. The settings for a serial port can be configured using the associated instance of Msvm_SerialPortSettingData.

classes.name: Msvm_SerialPortOnSerialController
classes.description: Associates a serial port with a serial controller.

properties.name: DebuggerMode
properties.description:
This property is set to TRUE if the debugger mode is enabled on a virtual serial port.
The debugger mode enhances the use of a Microsoft Windows kernel debugger over a virtual serial port.

classes.name: Msvm_ServiceAffectsElement
classes.description: Associates a virtual computer system instance with the management service that controls its state.

classes.name: Msvm_ServiceOfVssComponent
classes.description: An association between an instance of Msvm_VssComponent and an instance of Msvm_VssService, that represents a service for performing operations on the VSS Component.

classes.description: Contains settings used during servicing operations.

classes.name: Msvm_SettingDataComponent
classes.description: An association used to establish relationships between one instance of Msvm_EmulatedEthernetPortSettingData and Msvm_SyntheticEthernetPortSettingData with instances of Msvm_GuestNetworkAdapterConfiguration.

properties.description: Reference to an instance of Msvm_EmulatedEthernetPortSettingData or Msvm_SyntheticEthernetPortSettingData representing an Ethernet port.

properties.name: PartComponent
properties.description: Reference to an instance of Msvm_GuestNetworkAdapterConfiguration representing guest network adapter configuration.

classes.name: Msvm_SettingsDefineCapabilities
classes.description: Provides a link between the capabilities instance and the minimum, maximum, incremental, and default settings for a resource.

properties.name: SupportStatement
properties.description: Identifies the support statement.

classes.name: Msvm_ShutdownComponent
classes.description: Represents the state of the shutdown component, which provides a mechanism to shut down the operating system of the virtual machine from the management interfaces on the host system.

methods.name: InitiateShutdown
methods.description:
Initiates an operating system shutdown operation on the associated child virtual machine.
If zero (0) is returned, then the shutdown was initiated successfully. Any other return code indicates an error condition.

parameters.name: RequestedState
parameters.description: The state requested for the element. This information will be placed into the RequestedState property of the instance if the return code of the RequestStateChange method is 0 ('Completed with No Error'), or 4096 (0x1000) ('Job Started'). Refer to the description of the EnabledState and RequestedState properties for the detailed explanations of the RequestedState values.

parameters.name: Job
parameters.description: May contain a reference to the ConcreteJob created to track the state transition initiated by the method invocation.

parameters.name: TimeoutPeriod
parameters.description:
A timeout period that specifies the maximum amount of time that the client expects the transition to the new state to take. The interval format must be used to specify the TimeoutPeriod. A value of 0 or a null parameter indicates that the client has no time requirements for the transition. 
If this property does not contain 0 or null and the implementation does not support this parameter, a return code of 'Use Of Timeout Parameter Not Supported' shall be returned.

methods.name: InitiateReboot
methods.description:
Initiates an operating system reboot operation on the associated child virtual machine.
If zero (0) is returned, then the reboot was initiated successfully. Any other return code indicates an error condition.

methods.name: InitiateHibernate
methods.description:
Initiates an operating system hibernate operation on the associated child virtual machine.
If zero (0) is returned, then the hibernate was initiated successfully. Any other return code indicates an error condition.

classes.name: Msvm_ShutdownComponentSettingData
classes.description:
Represents the configured state of the shutdown component.
The default state is Enabled.

properties.name: EnabledState
properties.description:
The enabled and disabled states of an element.
Valid values are 2 (enabled) and 3 (disabled). The default value is 2 (enabled).
This is a read-only property, but it can be changed using the ModifyVirtualSystemResources method of the Msvm_VirtualSystemManagementService class.

classes.name: Msvm_SnapshotCollection
classes.description: Represents a collection of virtual system snapshots.

qualifiers.Description: The unique identification of the Collection object.

properties.description: The unique identification of the Collection object.

properties.name: ElementName
properties.description: An user-defined name for the collection. Note this is not guaranteed to be unique.

classes.name: Msvm_SnapshotOfVirtualSystem
classes.description: The CIM_SnapshotOfVirtuaSystem association associates an instance of the CIM_ComputerSystem class representing a virtual system, and an instance of the CIM_VirtualSystemSettingData class representing a snapshot that was captured from the virtual system.

classes.name: Msvm_SnapshotOfVirtualSystemCollection
classes.description: Associates the Msvm_SnapshotCollection to the corresponding Msvm_VirtualSystemCollection objects.

properties.name: Antecedent

qualifiers.Description: Antecedent represents the independent object in this association.

properties.description: Antecedent represents the independent object in this association.
