classes.name: Msvm_StorageAlert
classes.description: An alert generated by a virtual storage resource.

classes.name: Msvm_StorageAllocationSettingData
classes.description: Represents settings specifically related to the allocation of virtual storage.

properties.name: IOPSLimit
properties.description: The maximum number of I/O operations per second which will be serviced for this virtual storage extent. If the value is not defined or 0 there is no limit to the number of IOPS that the device can issue.This property is expressed in normalized I/Os per second. Each I/O request is accounted for as 1 normalized I/O if the size of the request is less than or equal to a predefined base size (8KB). Requests that are larger than the base size are accounted for as N I/Os, where N is the rounded-up value of the request size divided by the base size (for example, if the base size is 8KB, a 32KB requests is counted as 4 normalized I/Os, a 60KB request as 8 normalized I/Os, etc...).

properties.name: IOPSReservation
properties.description: The minimum number of I/O operations per second which will be serviced for this virtual storage extent. If both IOPSLimit and IOPSReservation are defined, the value of IOPSLimit must be greater or equal to IOPSReservation. This property is expressed in normalized I/Os per second. Each I/O request is accounted for as 1 normalized I/O if the size of the request is less than or equal to a predefined base size (8KB). Requests that are larger than the base size are accounted for as N I/Os, where N is the rounded-up value of the request size divided by the base size (for example, if the base size is 8KB, a 32KB requests is counted as 4 normalized I/Os, a 64KB request as 8 normalized I/Os, etc...).

properties.name: IOPSAllocationUnits
properties.description: Specifies the allocation units used by the IOPSLimit and IOPSReservation properties.

properties.name: PersistentReservationsSupported
properties.description: Indicates whether the virtual hard disk supports SCSI-3 persistent reservations

properties.name: StorageQoSPolicyID
properties.description: Specifies the unique identifier of the Storage QoS Policy to be applied to this virtual storage extent.

properties.name: SnapshotId
properties.description: A GUID representing which snapshot within the VHD Set file is to be attached.

properties.name: CachingMode
properties.description: Indicates whether and how in-memory file caching should be used for this VHD. The default policy is set in the DefaultVirtualHardDiskCachingMode field of the Msvm_VirtualSystemManagementServiceSettingData class. This property is reserved for system use.

properties.name: IgnoreFlushes
properties.description: Specifies whether requests from the VM to flush or write through the cache are respected. If true, then the host ignores all flush or write through requests.

properties.name: WriteHardeningMethod
properties.description: Indicates what write hardening method is supported by the disk.

classes.name: Msvm_StorageJob
classes.description: This class represents a image operation job created by the Microsoft Hyper-V Image Management Service.

properties.name: Cancellable
properties.description: Indicates whether the job can be cancelled. The value of this property does not guarantee that a request to cancel the job will succeed.

properties.name: Child
properties.description: On failure of the asynchronous operation, this property contains the file path to the child of the virtual hard drive being affected by this operation.

properties.name: JobCompletionStatusCode
properties.description: The HRESULT code that describes the completion status for the asynchronous operation.

properties.name: Parent
properties.description: On failure of the asynchronous operation, this property contains the file path to the parent of the virtual hard drive being affected by this operation.

properties.name: JobType
properties.description:
The type of asynchronous operation being tracked by this instance of Msvm_StorageJob.
Values are:
Unknown
VHD Creation: Creating a virtual hard disk image (VHD).
Floppy Creation: Creating a virtual floppy disk image (VFD).
Compaction: Compacting the size of a virtual hard disk image.
Expansion: Expanding the size of a virtual hard disk image.
Merging: Merging multiple virtual hard disk images into a single image.
Conversion: Converting the type of a virtual hard disk image.
Loopback Mount: Mounting the virtual hard disk on the parent partition.
Get VHD Info: Retrieving information about a virtual hard disk image (VHD).
Validate VHD Image: Validating a virtual hard disk image (VHD).

parameters.name: RequestedState
parameters.description:
RequestStateChange changes the state of a job. The possible values are as follows: 
Start (2) changes the state to 'Running'. 
Suspend (3) stops the job temporarily. The intention is to subsequently restart the job with 'Start'. It might be possible to enter the 'Service' state while suspended. (This is job-specific.) 
Terminate (4) stops the job cleanly, saving data, preserving the state, and shutting down all underlying processes in an orderly manner. 
Kill (5) terminates the job immediately with no requirement to save data or preserve the state. 
Service (6) puts the job into a vendor-specific service state. It might be possible to restart the job.

parameters.name: TimeoutPeriod
parameters.description:
A timeout period that specifies the maximum amount of time that the client expects the transition to the new state to take. The interval format must be used to specify the TimeoutPeriod. A value of 0 or a null parameter indicates that the client has no time requirements for the transition. 
If this property does not contain 0 or null and the implementation does not support this parameter, a return code of 'Use Of Timeout Parameter Not Supported' must be returned.

parameters.name: Error
parameters.description: If the OperationalStatus on the Job is not "OK", then this method will return a CIM Error instance. Otherwise, when the Job is "OK", null is returned.

methods.name: GetErrorEx
methods.description: When the job is executing or has terminated without error, then this method returns no Msvm_Error instance. However, if the job has failed because of some internal problem or because the job has been terminated by a client, then one or more Msvm_Error instances are returned.

classes.name: Msvm_StorageSettingData
classes.description: Represents the storage-specific settings for a virtual system.

properties.name: VirtualProcessorsPerChannel
properties.description:
The number of processors per storage channel. The number of channels to be opened is given by (Count of logical processors on the host /VirtualProcessorsPerChannel).
 This is a read-only property, but it can be changed using the ModifySystemComponentSettings method of the WmiMsvmVirtualSystemManagementService class.

properties.name: DisableInterruptBatching
properties.description:
The flag specifies whether interrupt batching should be disabled.
 This is a read-only property, but it can be changed using the ModifySystemComponentSettings method of the WmiMsvmVirtualSystemManagementService class.

properties.name: ThreadCountPerChannel
properties.description:
The number of threads per storage channel to process IO.
Default: Default behavior. 
Low: Low number of threads per storage channel.
Medium: Medium number of threads per storage channel.
High: High number of threads per storage channel.
This is a read-only property, but it can be changed using the ModifySystemComponentSettings method of the WmiMsvmVirtualSystemManagementService class.

classes.name: Msvm_SummaryInformation
classes.description: Used in the GetSummaryInformation method in the Msvm_VirtualSystemManagementService class to quickly retrieve common information related to a virtual system or snapshot.

properties.name: AllocatedGPU
properties.description: The identifier of the physical graphics processing unit (GPU) allocated to this virtual machine (VM). This property only applies to VMs that use RemoteFX.

properties.name: AsynchronousTasks
properties.description: An array of Msvm_ConcreteJob instances representing any asynchronous operations related to the virtual system which are currently executing. This property is not valid for instances of Msvm_SummaryInformation representing a virtual system snapshot.

properties.name: CreationTime
properties.description: The time at which the virtual system or snapshot was created.

properties.name: ElementName
properties.description: The friendly name for the virtual system or snapshot.

properties.name: EnabledState
properties.description: The current state of the virtual system or snapshot.

properties.name: OtherEnabledState
properties.description: A string that describes the enabled or disabled state of the element when the EnabledState property is set to 1 ("Other"). This property must be set to null when EnabledState is any value other than 1.

properties.name: GuestOperatingSystem
properties.description: The name of the guest operating system, if available. If this information is not available, the value of this property is NULL. This property is not valid for instances of Msvm_SummaryInformation representing a virtual system snapshot.

properties.name: HealthState
properties.description: The current health state for the virtual system. This property is not valid for instances of Msvm_SummaryInformation representing a virtual system snapshot.

properties.name: Heartbeat
properties.description: The current heartbeat status for the virtual system. This property may be one of the following values: "OK"; "Error"; "No Contact"; or "Lost Communication". For more information, see the documentation for the StatusDescriptions property of the Msvm_HeartbeatComponent class. This property is not valid for instances of Msvm_SummaryInformation representing a virtual system snapshot.

properties.name: MemoryUsage
properties.description: The current memory usage of the virtual system. This property is not valid for instances of Msvm_SummaryInformation representing a virtual system snapshot.

properties.name: MemoryAvailable
properties.description: The memory available percentage in the virtual system.

properties.name: AvailableMemoryBuffer
properties.description: The available memory buffer percentage in the virtual system.

properties.name: SwapFilesInUse
properties.description: Indicates if Smart Paging is active.

properties.name: Name
properties.description: The unique name for the virtual system or snapshot.

properties.name: Notes
properties.description: The notes associated with the virtual system or snapshot.

properties.name: NumberOfProcessors
properties.description: The total number of virtual processors allocated to the virtual system or snapshot.

properties.name: OperationalStatus
properties.description: The current status of the element.

properties.name: ProcessorLoad
properties.description: The current processor usage of the virtual system. This property is not valid for instances of Msvm_SummaryInformation representing a virtual system snapshot.

properties.name: ProcessorLoadHistory
properties.description: An array of the previous 100 samples of the processor usage for the virtual system. This property is not valid for instances of Msvm_SummaryInformation representing a virtual system snapshot.

properties.name: Snapshots
properties.description: An array of Msvm_VirtualSystemSettingData instances representing the snapshots for the virtual system. This property is not valid for instances of Msvm_SummaryInformation representing a virtual system snapshot.

properties.name: StatusDescriptions
properties.description: Strings that describe the various OperationalStatus array values.

properties.name: ThumbnailImage
properties.description: An array containing a small, thumbnail-sized image of the desktop for the virtual system or snapshot in RGB565 format.

properties.name: UpTime
properties.description: The amount of time since the virtual system was last booted. This property is not valid for instances of Msvm_SummaryInformation representing a virtual system snapshot.

properties.name: ReplicationState
properties.description: Replication state for the virtual machine.

properties.name: ReplicationHealth
properties.description: Replication health for the virtual machine.

properties.name: ReplicationMode
properties.description: Identifies replication type for the virtual machine.

properties.name: TestReplicaSystem
properties.description: Reference to the CIM_ComputerSystem instance representing the test replica virtual system for the virtual machine. This property is not valid for instances of Msvm_SummaryInformation representing a virtual system snapshot.

properties.name: ApplicationHealth
properties.description: The current application health status for the virtual system. This property may be one of the following values: "OK"; "Application Critical"; "Disabled".For more information, see the documentation for the StatusDescriptions property of the Msvm_HeartbeatComponent class. This property is not valid for instances of Msvm_SummaryInformation representing a virtual system snapshot.

properties.name: IntegrationServicesVersionState
properties.description: Whether or not the integration services installed in the virtual machine are up to date

properties.name: MemorySpansPhysicalNumaNodes
properties.description: Indicates whether or not the memory of the one or more of the virtual non-uniform memory access (NUMA) nodes of the virtual machine spans multiple physical NUMA nodes of the hosting computer system.

properties.name: ReplicationStateEx
properties.description: The array of Replication state values for the various replication relationships of the virtual machine.

properties.name: ReplicationHealthEx
properties.description: The array of Replication health values for the various replication relationships of the virtual machine.

properties.name: ReplicationProviderId
properties.description:
Globally unique identifier of provider that identifies the endpoint provider. For Hyper-V host to another Hyper-V host, provider id is fixed as 22391CDC-272C-4DDF-BA88-9BEFB1A0975C.
In case of external provider this is CLSID of provider COM class object.

properties.name: EnhancedSessionModeState
properties.description: Indicates whether or not enhanced mode connections are allowed by the host and if allowed, whether or not they are available to the virtual machine.

properties.name: VirtualSwitchNames
properties.description: Strings that list the friendly names of the virtual switches the VM is connected to.

properties.name: VirtualSystemSubType
properties.description: The subtype of the virtual system.

properties.name: InstanceID

qualifiers.Description:
InstanceID is an optional property that may be used to opaquely and uniquely identify an instance of this class within the scope of the instantiating Namespace. Various subclasses of this class may override this property to make it required, or a key. Such subclasses may also modify the preferred algorithms for ensuring uniqueness that are defined below.
To ensure uniqueness within the NameSpace, the value of InstanceID should be constructed using the following "preferred" algorithm: 
<OrgID>:<LocalID> 
Where <OrgID> and <LocalID> are separated by a colon (:), and where <OrgID> must include a copyrighted, trademarked, or otherwise unique name that is owned by the business entity that is creating or defining the InstanceID or that is a registered ID assigned to the business entity by a recognized global authority. (This requirement is similar to the <Schema Name>_<Class Name> structure of Schema class names.) In addition, to ensure uniqueness, <OrgID> must not contain a colon (:). When using this algorithm, the first colon to appear in InstanceID must appear between <OrgID> and <LocalID>. 
<LocalID> is chosen by the business entity and should not be reused to identify different underlying (real-world) elements. If not null and the above "preferred" algorithm is not used, the defining entity must assure that the resulting InstanceID is not reused across any InstanceIDs produced by this or other providers for the NameSpace of this instance. 
If not set to null for DMTF-defined instances, the "preferred" algorithm must be used with the <OrgID> set to CIM.

properties.name: Version
properties.description: The version of the virtual system in a format of "major.minor", for example "2.0".

properties.name: ThumbnailImageWidth
properties.description: The width in pixels of the image in the ThumbnailImage property.

properties.name: HostComputerSystemName
properties.description: The name of the computer hosting this virtual machine.

properties.name: Shielded

properties.name: HypervisorPartitionId
properties.description: The unique identifier of the hypervisor partition used by the virtual system.

properties.name: AllowPacketDirect

properties.name: InterruptModeration

properties.name: MediaType

properties.name: NumaAwarePlacement

properties.name: AllowDirectTranslatedP2P

classes.name: Msvm_SyntheticMouse
classes.description: Represents a synthetic mouse device.

properties.name: AbsoluteCoordinates
properties.description: Indicates whether the device operates on absolute coordinates. If not set, the device's coordinates are relative. This property is set to True.

properties.name: HorizontalPosition
properties.description: The absolute x-coordinate of the pointing device.

properties.name: ScrollPosition
properties.description: The z-coordinate position of the mouse device.

properties.name: VerticalPosition
properties.description: The absolute y-coordinate of the pointing device.

methods.name: GetButtonState
methods.description:
Retrieves the current state of the specified device button.
Return value: A return value of zero indicates success. A non-zero value indicates a query failure.

methods.name: SetButtonState
methods.description:
Sets the current state of the specified device button.
Return value: Non zero values indicate a failure to modify the button state.

methods.name: ClickButton
methods.description:
Simulates a button click, a down-up sequence, on the specified device button.
Return value: A return value of zero indicates success. A non-zero value indicates a failure to modify the button state.

methods.name: SetAbsolutePosition
methods.description:
Offsets the position of the mouse by the specified horizontal and vertical deltas.
Return value: A return value of zero indicates success. A non-zero value indicates a failure to alter the scroll position.

methods.name: SetScrollPosition
methods.description:
Adjusts the z-coordinate of the wheel control of the pointing device. Values written to this property are always relative coordinate offsets.
Return value: A return value of zero indicates success. A non-zero value indicates a failure to alter the scroll position.

The default Enabled State is Disabled.

properties.name: Shielded
properties.description:
A policy to define whether a virtual machine is shielded.
This property is set to TRUE if a VM is shielded.
A newly created TPM is disabled so the initial shielding state is FALSE.

properties.name: DataProtected
properties.description:
A policy to define whether a virtual machine's data is protected.
This property is set to TRUE if a VM will have its data protected.
A newly created TPM is disabled so the initial data protection state is FALSE.

properties.name: EnabledState
properties.description:
The enabled and disabled states of an element.
Valid values are 2 (enabled) and 3 (disabled). The default value is 3 (disabled).

properties.name: KeyProtector

properties.name: LastKnownGoodKeyProtector
properties.description:
Last Known Good Key Protector successfully encrypted TPM device state in last VM boot.
It is read-only for WMI client and can only be modified by VM TPM device.

properties.description: The path of the file represented by this snapshot. This field may be empty if there is no file associated with this snapshot.

properties.name: ParentPathsList
properties.description: A list of file paths representing all of the files on which this snapshot depends. This field will be empty unless specifically requested. The first entry is the file's immediate parent, with the last entry being the root.

properties.name: CreationTime
properties.description: The date and time of this snapshot's creation.

properties.name: ResilientChangeTrackingId
properties.description: The Resilient Change Tracking Id, if any, associated with this snapshot.

classes.name: Msvm_VirtualEthernetSwitchManagementService
classes.description: Represents the virtualization service present on a single host system. Msvm_VirtualEthernetSwitchManagementService is used to control the definition, modification, and deletion of virtual Ethernet switches.

methods.name: DefineSystem
methods.description:
Defines a virtual system.
Input that is not completely specified may be filled out with default values.

parameters.name: SystemSettings
parameters.description: String containing an embedded instance of class CIM_VirtualSystemSettingData that is used to define attributes of the virtual system to be created.

parameters.name: ResourceSettings
parameters.description: Array of strings each containing an embedded instance of class CIM_ResourceAllocationSettingData that describes the virtual aspects of a virtual resource to be created in the scope of the new virtual system.

parameters.name: ReferenceConfiguration
parameters.description: Reference to an instance of class CIM_VirtualSystemSettingData object that is the top level object of a reference virtual system configuration. The reference configuration is used to complement the configuration of the new virtual system if parameters SystemSettings and ResourceSettings did not provide respective information.

parameters.name: ResultingSystem
parameters.description: If a virtual computer system is successfully defined, a reference to an instance of class CIM_ComputerSystem that represents the newly defined virtual computer system is returned.

parameters.name: Job
parameters.description: If the operation is long running, then optionally a job may be returned. In this case, the instance of class CIM_ComputerSystem representing the new virtual systemis presented via association CIM_AffectedJobElementwith property AffectedElement refering to the new instance of class CIM_ComputerSystem and property ElementEffects set to 5 (Create).

methods.name: DestroySystem
methods.description:
Destroys a virtual system.
The referenced virtual system is destroyed, including any elements scoped by it. Virtual resources are returned to their resource pools, which may imply the destruction of those resources (implementation dependent). If the virtual system is active when the operation is invoked, it is first deactivated and then destroyed. If snapshots were created from the virtual system, these are destroyed as well.

parameters.name: AffectedSystem
parameters.description: Reference to an instance of class CIM_ComputerSystem representing the virtual computer system that it to be destroyed.

parameters.name: Job
parameters.description: If the operation is long running, then optionally a job may be returned.

methods.name: ModifySystemSettings
methods.description:
Modifies virtual system settings.
When applied to the system settings of a "current" virtual system configuration, as a side effect the virtual system instance may be modified.

parameters.name: SystemSettings
parameters.description: String containing an instance of class CIM_VirtualSystemSettingData that is used to modify the settings of the virtual system. The instance must have a valid InstanceID in order to identify the virtual system setting to be modified.

parameters.name: Job
parameters.description: If the operation is long running, then optionally a job may be returned.

methods.name: AddResourceSettings
methods.description:
Adds resources to a virtual system configuration
.When applied to a "state" virtual system configuration, as a side effect resources are added to the active virtual system.

parameters.name: AffectedConfiguration
parameters.description: Reference to the affected virtual system configuration.

parameters.name: ResourceSettings
parameters.description: Array of strings each containing one embedded instance of class CIM_ResourceAllocationSettingData that describes the virtual aspects of a virtual resource to be added to the virtual system.

parameters.name: ResultingResourceSettings
parameters.description: Array of references to instances of class CIM_ResourceAllocationSettingData representing virtual aspects of the added virtual resources.

parameters.name: Job
parameters.description: If the operation is long running, then optionally a job may be returned. In this case, the instances of class CIM_ResourceAllocationSettingData representing the added resource settings are available via association CIM_ConreteComponent from the instance of class CIM_VirtualSystemSettingData representing the affected virtual system configuration.

methods.name: ModifyResourceSettings
methods.description:
Modifies virtual resource settings.
When applied to parts of a "current" virtual system configuration, as a side effect resources of the active virtual system may be modified.

parameters.name: ResourceSettings
parameters.description: Array of strings each containing an embedded instance of class CIM_ResourceAllocationSettingData that describes modifications to the virtual aspects of an existing virtual resource. parameters.name: RequestedState
parameters.description: The state requested for the element. This information will be placed into the RequestedState property of the instance if the return code of the RequestStateChange method is 0 ('Completed with No Error'), or 4096 (0x1000) ('Job Started'). Refer to the description of the EnabledState and RequestedState properties for the detailed explanations of the RequestedState values.

parameters.name: Job
parameters.description: May contain a reference to the ConcreteJob created to track the state transition initiated by the method invocation.

parameters.name: TimeoutPeriod
parameters.description:
A timeout period that specifies the maximum amount of time that the client expects the transition to the new state to take. The interval format must be used to specify the TimeoutPeriod. A value of 0 or a null parameter indicates that the client has no time requirements for the transition. 
If this property does not contain 0 or null and the implementation does not support this parameter, a return code of 'Use Of Timeout Parameter Not Supported' shall be returned.

properties.name: IsPmemCompatible

properties.name: LastApplyConsistencyLevel
properties.description:
Consistency level of the last applied delta.
Application Consistent: The last applied delta indicates a point in time when the virtual system was in application consistent state. 
Crash Consistent: The last applied delta indicates a point in time when the virtual system was in crash consistent state. 
Group Crash Consistent: The last applied delta indicates a point in time when the group was in crash consistent state.

classes.name: Msvm_VirtualSystemExportSettingData
classes.description: Provides additional information to be used with the ExportVirtualSystemEx method of the Msvm_VirtualSystemManagementService class.

properties.name: CopySnapshotConfiguration
properties.description:
Indicates what snapshots are to be exported with the VM.
ExportAllSnapshots = All snapshots will be exported with the VM.
ExportNoSnapshots = No snapshots will be exported with the VM.
ExportOneSnapshot = The snapshots identified by the SnapshotVirtualSystem property will be exported with the VM. The CopyVmStorage and CopyVmRuntimeInformation properties are ignored and storage and runtime information is exported with the VM and any VHD differencing disks will be merged into a new VHD.
ExportOneSnapshotForBackup = The snapshot identified by the SnapshotVirtualSystem property will be exported for the purpose of backing up the VM. The exported configuration will use ID of the VM.

properties.name: CopyVmRuntimeInformation
properties.description:
Indicates whether the VM runtime information will be copied when the VM is exported.
If TRUE, the VM runtime information will be copied.

properties.name: CopyVmStorage
properties.description:
Indicates whether the VM storage will be copied when the VM is exported.
If TRUE, the VM storage will be copied.

properties.name: CreateVmExportSubdirectory
properties.description:
Indicates whether a subdirectory with the name of the VM will be created when the VM is exported.
If TRUE, a subdirectory will be created.

properties.name: SnapshotVirtualSystem
properties.description: Path to a Msvm_VirtualSystemSettingData instance that represents the snapshot to be exported with the VM. If the CopySnapshotConfiguration property is not set to 2 (ExportOneSnapshot) or 3(ExportOneSnapshotForBackup), this property is ignored.

properties.name: CaptureLiveState
properties.description:
Indicates what state to capture if the target of the export is a running VM.
CaptureCrashConsistentState = No saved state files will be exported for the running VM, placing it in a crash consistent state.
CaptureSavedState = Saved state files for the running VM will be exported along with the VM configuration.
CaptureAppConsistentState = Application-consistent state of the runningVM will be exported.
CaptureGuestDebugState = Debuggable state of the running VM will be exported.
CaptureManagementVtlState = Debuggable state of the management VTL of the runningVM will be exported.
CaptureNoHaltMemory = The memory state of the running VM will be exported without suspending the VM.

properties.name: DifferentialBackupBase
properties.description: Base for differential export. This is either path to a Msvm_VirtualSystemReferencePoint instance that represents the reference point or path to a Msvm_VirtualSystemSettingData instance that represents the snapshot to be used as a base for differential export. If the CopySnapshotConfiguration property is not set to 3(ExportOneSnapshotForBackup), this property is ignored.

properties.name: BackupIntent
properties.description:
Indicates the intent on how the exported backup sets would be used
BackupIntentPreserveChain = All exported full and differential backup sets would be preserved as they are.
BackupIntentMerge = The exported full and differential backup sets would be merged to synthesize full backup sets.

properties.name: ExportForLiveMigration
properties.description: Indicates whether the exported VM is intended to be used in live migration.

properties.name: ExcludedVirtualHardDisks
properties.description:
Array of Msvm_StorageAllocationSettingData (RASD) instance IDs that represent the virtual hard disks that are requested to be excluded from the export operation. If at least one of the supplied IDs is not a valid attached virtual hard disk, the operation will fail.
The virtual hard disks referenced by this property may be from the VM and/or from any of its snapshots. Exclusion of virtual hard disks is not supported when property CopySnapshotConfiguration is set to  0(ExportAllSnapshots).
Note that the RASD instance ID for virtual hard disks represents the location they are attached to, and excluding through this ID excludes all virtual hard disks attached in that location throughout the virtual machine's snapshot tree, regardless of them really being a valid virtual hard disk chain.

properties.name: DisableDifferentialOfIgnoredStorage
properties.description: Indicates whether differencing disks will be created or not for storage ignored during export. By default this is set to false, which means that differencing disks are created for the storage that is not going to be copied over to the export destination.

properties.name: GuestDebugStateFilePath
properties.description:
Indicates the file path where the guest debug state of the VM will be placed.
This setting requires CaptureSavedState to be set to 'CaptureGuestDebugState'. When this path is supplied, all virtual disks, guest state file and VM configuration file are ignored. This essentially makes it possible to export only a saved state file with debuggable state of a VM. This is not importable.

classes.name: Msvm_VirtualSystemManagementService
classes.description: Represents the virtualization service present on a single host system. Msvm_VirtualSystemManagementService is used to control the definition, modification, and deletion of virtual systems. It also has methods for performing operations on virtual systems, such as cloning, snapshotting, and the importing or exporting of virtual systems. To retrieve per-VM information, use Msvm_ComputerSystem and Msvm_PlannedComputerSystem.

methods.name: DefineSystem
methods.description:
Defines a virtual system.
Input that is not completely specified may be filled out with default values.

parameters.name: SystemSettings
parameters.description: String containing an embedded instance of class CIM_VirtualSystemSettingData that is used to define attributes of the virtual system to be created.

parameters.name: ResourceSettings
parameters.description: Array of strings each containing an embedded instance of class CIM_ResourceAllocationSettingData that describes the virtual aspects of a virtual resource to be created in the scope of the new virtual system.

parameters.name: ReferenceConfiguration
parameters.description: Reference to an instance of class CIM_VirtualSystemSettingData object that is the top level object of a reference virtual system configuration. The reference configuration is used to complement the configuration of the new virtual system if parameters SystemSettings and ResourceSettings did not provide respective information.

parameters.name: ResultingSystem
parameters.description: If a virtual computer system is successfully defined, a reference to an instance of class CIM_ComputerSystem that represents the newly defined virtual computer system is returned.

parameters.name: Job
parameters.description: If the operation is long running, then optionally a job may be returned. In this case, the instance of class CIM_ComputerSystem representing the new virtual systemis presented via association CIM_AffectedJobElementwith property AffectedElement refering to the new instance of class CIM_ComputerSystem and property ElementEffects set to 5 (Create).

methods.name: DestroySystem
methods.description:
Destroys a virtual system.
The referenced virtual system is destroyed, including any elements scoped by it. Virtual resources are returned to their resource pools, which may imply the destruction of those resources (implementation dependent). If the virtual system is active when the operation is invoked, it is first deactivated and then destroyed. If snapshots were created from the virtual system, these are destroyed as well.

parameters.name: AffectedSystem
parameters.description: Reference to an instance of class CIM_ComputerSystem representing the virtual computer system that it to be destroyed.

parameters.name: Job
parameters.description: If the operation is long running, then optionally a job may be returned.

methods.name: ModifySystemSettings
methods.description:
Modifies virtual system settings.
When applied to the system settings of a "current" virtual system configuration, as a side effect the virtual system instance may be modified.

parameters.name: SystemSettings
parameters.description: String containing an instance of class CIM_VirtualSystemSettingData that is used to modify the settings of the virtual system. The instance must have a valid InstanceID in order to identify the virtual system setting to be modified.

parameters.name: Job
parameters.description: If the operation is long running, then optionally a job may be returned.

methods.name: ImportSystemDefinition
methods.description: Creates a new planned computer system based on the specified virtual system definition.  The specified definition files must not already be in use by the host system or the virtualization platform.  The SnapshotFolder folder will be searched in order to locate any snapshots referenced by the virtual system definition.  Any referenced snapshots not found in this location must be deleted using the RemoveVirtualSystemSnapshot method, or imported using the ImportSnapshotDefinitions method prior to realizing the planned computer system.

methods.name: ImportSnapshotDefinitions
methods.description: Searches the SnapshotFolder folder for any snapshot definition files associated with the specified planned computer system.  A new snapshot is created on the planned computer system for every associated definition file in this location.

methods.name: RealizePlannedSystem
methods.description: Validates the configuration of a planned virtual system and converts it to a realized virtual system.  Any runtime or saved state files associated with the virtual system will be copied from the import directory to the virtual system's data root, if applicable.

methods.name: ExportSystemDefinition
methods.description:
Exports a virtual computer system, or a snapshot of a virtual computer system, in the "powered off" or "saved" state to a file. The virtual computer system, its associated configuration settings, and its associated resource settings will be preserved in the resulting file.
Return value: If this method is executed synchronously, it returns 0 if it succeeds. If this method is executed asynchronously, it returns 4096 and the Job output parameter can be used to track the progress of the asynchronous operation. Any other return value indicates an error.

parameters.name: TimeoutPeriod
parameters.description:
A timeout period that specifies the maximum amount of time that the client expects the transition to the new state to take. The interval format must be used to specify the TimeoutPeriod. A value of 0 or a null parameter indicates that the client has no time requirements for the transition. 
If this property does not contain 0 or null and the implementation does not support this parameter, a return code of 'Use Of Timeout Parameter Not Supported' shall be returned.

methods.name: ModifyDiskMergeSettings
methods.description: Modifies the disk merge setting data.

methods.name: GenerateWwpn
methods.description:
This API generates a requested number of WorldWidePortNames from within a preconfigured range defined by MinimumWwpnAddress and MaximumWwpnAddress properties of class Msvm_VirtualSystemManagementSettingData.
The NumberOfWwpns requested per call must not be less than 1 or more than 1024.
If the valid number of WWPNs that can be generated is less than the requested number, then the remaining entries in the GeneratedWwpn array will have the invalid entry of "0000000000000000" and the return value will indicate success (0).

methods.name: AddFibreChannelChap
methods.description: This API adds DH-CHAP parameters to a synthetic fibre channel port in a VM. This will fail if the VM is running.

parameters.name: FcPortSettings
parameters.description: Array of strings each containing an embedded instance of class Msvm_SyntheticFcPortSettingData that describes settings for synthetic fibre channel ports for virtual machines.All instances must have a valid InstanceID in order to identify the feature settings to be modified.

methods.name: RemoveFibreChannelChap
methods.description: This API removes DH-CHAP parameters to a synthetic fibre channel port in a VM. This will fail if the VM is running.

parameters.name: FcPortSettings
parameters.description: Array of strings each containing an embedded instance of class Msvm_SyntheticFcPortSettingData that describes settings for synthetic fibre channel ports for virtual machines.All instances must have a valid InstanceID in order to identify the feature settings to be modified.

methods.name: SetGuestNetworkAdapterConfiguration
methods.description: Configures the network adapters within the guest operating system. These configuration parameters are applied immediately upon establishing communication with the KVP Exchange integration component running within the guest operating system.

methods.name: GetSizeOfSystemFiles
methods.description:
Retrieves the total size of the system files.
These include the configuration and saved state files.

methods.name: GetCurrentWwpnFromGenerator
methods.description:
This API provides the ability to see the current WorldWidePortName from within a preconfigured range defined by MinimumWwpnAddress and MaximumWwpnAddress properties of class Msvm_VirtualSystemManagementSettingData.
If the range as defined by these properties is exhausted, then the CurrentWwpn out parameter will have the invalid entry of "0000000000000000" and the return value will indicate success (0).

methods.name: TestNetworkConnection
methods.description: Tests the network connectivity of a VM in a Windows Network Virtualization Environment.

parameters.name: TargetNetworkAdapter
parameters.description: Reference to the Ethernet connection.

parameters.name: RoundTripTime
parameters.description: The round trip time for the Ping request.

parameters.name: Job
parameters.description: If the operation is long running, then optionally a job may be returned.

methods.name: DefinePlannedSystem
methods.description:
Defines a planned virtual system.
Input that is not completely specified may be filled out with default values.

parameters.name: SystemSettings
parameters.description: String containing an embedded instance of class CIM_VirtualSystemSettingData that is used to define attributes of the virtual system to be created.

parameters.name: ResourceSettings
parameters.description: Array of strings each containing an embedded instance of class CIM_ResourceAllocationSettingData that describes the virtual aspects of a virtual resource to be created in the scope of the new virtual system.

parameters.name: ReferenceConfiguration
parameters.description: Reference to an instance of class CIM_VirtualSystemSettingData object that is the top level object of a reference virtual system configuration. The reference configuration is used to complement the configuration of the new virtual system if parameters SystemSettings and ResourceSettings did not provide respective information.

parameters.name: ResultingSystem
parameters.description: If a virtual computer system is successfully defined, a reference to an instance of class CIM_ComputerSystem that represents the newly defined virtual computer system is returned.

parameters.name: Job
parameters.description: If the operation is long running, then optionally a job may be returned. In this case, the instance of class CIM_ComputerSystem representing the new virtual systemis presented via association CIM_AffectedJobElementwith property AffectedElement refering to the new instance of class CIM_ComputerSystem and property ElementEffects set to 5 (Create).

methods.name: UpgradeSystemVersion
methods.description:
Upgrades the virtual system.
When applied to the system settings of a "current" virtual system configuration

parameters.name: Job

methods.name: AddGuestServiceSettings
methods.description:
Adds guest service settings to a virtual system configuration.
When applied to parts of a "current" virtual system configuration, as a side effect guest services of the active virtual system may be modified.

parameters.name: AffectedConfiguration

parameters.name: GuestServiceSettings

parameters.name: ResultingGuestServiceSettings

In this case, the instances of class CIM_SettingData representing the added guest service settings are available via association CIM_ConreteComponent from the instance of class CIM_VirtualSystemSettingData representing the affected virtual system configuration.

methods.name: ModifyGuestServiceSettings
methods.description:
Modifies guest service settings.
When applied to parts of a "current" virtual system configuration, as a side effect guest services of the active virtual system may be modified.

In this case, the instances of class CIM_SettingData representing the modified guest service settings are available via association CIM_ConreteComponent from the instance of class CIM_VirtualSystemSettingData representing the affected virtual system configuration.

Removes virtual resource settings from a virtual system configuration.
When applied to parts of a "current" virtual system configuration, as a side effect resources of the active virtual system may be removed.

parameters.name: Job

classes.name: Msvm_VirtualSystemManagementServiceSettingData
classes.description: Represents the settings for the virtualization service present on a single host system. The properties for this class cannot be modified directly. The client must call the Msvm_VirtualSystemManagementService.ModifyServiceSettings method to modify any of these properties.

properties.name: BiosLockString
properties.description:
Used by OEMs to allow BIOS-locked Windows operating systems to run in the virtual machine. This string must be exactly 32 characters in length.
This is a read-only property, but it can be changed using the ModifyServiceSettings method of the Msvm_VirtualSystemManagementService class.

properties.name: DefaultExternalDataRoot
properties.description:
The default external data root. By default, "root\ProgramData\Microsoft\Windows\Virtualization".
This is a read-only property, but it can be changed using the ModifyServiceSettings method of the Msvm_VirtualSystemManagementService class.

properties.name: DefaultVirtualHardDiskPath
properties.description:
The default virtual hard disk path. By default, "root\ProgramData\Microsoft\Windows\Virtual Hard Disks".
This is a read-only property, but it can be changed using the ModifyServiceSettings method of the Msvm_VirtualSystemManagementService class.

properties.name: MaximumMacAddress
properties.description:
The maximum MAC address for dynamically generated MAC addresses.
This is a read-only property, but it can be changed using the ModifyServiceSettings method of the Msvm_VirtualSystemManagementService class.

properties.name: MinimumMacAddress
properties.description:
The minimum MAC address for dynamically generated MAC addresses.
This is a read-only property, but it can be changed using the ModifyServiceSettings method of the Msvm_VirtualSystemManagementService class.

properties.name: NumaSpanningEnabled
properties.description: Reserved for future use.

properties.name: PrimaryOwnerContact
properties.description:
Controls memory allocation for the VMs on non-uniform memory access (NUMA) systems.
This is a read-only property, but it can be changed using the ModifyServiceSettings method of the Msvm_VirtualSystemManagementService class.

properties.name: PrimaryOwnerName
properties.description:
Describes how the primary system owner can be reached (for example, phone number or e-mail address). By default, empty. This name may not exceed 256 characters in length.
This is a read-only property, but it can be changed using the ModifyServiceSettings method of the Msvm_VirtualSystemManagementService class.

properties.name: HbaLunTimeout
properties.description:
This property describes the amount of time that the Synthetic FC virtual device will wait for a LUN to appear before starting a virtual machine.
This is a read-only property, but it can be changed using the ModifyServiceSettings method of the Msvm_VirtualSystemManagementService class.

properties.name: MaximumWWPNAddress
properties.description:
The maximum WorldWidePortName address for dynamically generated WorldWideName addresses used for Synthetic HBAs.
This is a read-only property, but it can be changed using the ModifyServiceSettings method of the Msvm_VirtualSystemManagementService class.

properties.name: MinimumWWPNAddress
properties.description:
The minimum WorldWidePortName address for dynamically generated WorldWideName addresses used for Synthetic HBAs.
This is a read-only property, but it can be changed using the ModifyServiceSettings method of the Msvm_VirtualSystemManagementService class.

properties.name: CurrentWWNNAddress
properties.description:
The WorldWideNodeName address for dynamically generated WorldWideName addresses used for Synthetic HBAs.
This is a read-only property, but it can be changed using the ModifyServiceSettings method of the Msvm_VirtualSystemManagementService class.

properties.name: EnhancedSessionModeEnabled
properties.description:
Indicates whether enhanced session mode is allowed.
This is a read-only property, but it can be changed using the ModifyServiceSettings method of the Msvm_VirtualSystemManagementService class.

properties.name: DefaultVirtualHardDiskCachingMode
properties.description: Indicates whether in-memory file caching should be used for disks by default. This value can be overridden on a per-disk basis in the CachingMode field of the Msvm_StorageAllocationSettingData class. This property is reserved for system use.

properties.name: HypervisorRootSchedulerEnabled
properties.description: Whether or not the hypervisor root scheduler is enabled.

properties.name: HypervisorSnpStatus
properties.description: Indicates the hypervisor SNP feature status.

properties.name: HypervisorTdxStatus
properties.description: Indicates the hypervisor TDX feature status.

properties.name: HypervisorRmeStatus
properties.description: Indicates the hypervisor RME feature status.

classes.name: Msvm_VirtualSystemMigrationCapabilities
classes.description: Defines the means by which a client can discover the methods provided by the migration service, and valid range of virtual system migration setting data. The VirtualSystemMigrationCapabilities object is associated with VirtualSystemMigrationSettingData objects. These associations define the valid range of migration services available.

properties.name: AsynchronousMethodsSupported

qualifiers.Description: Enumeration of method identifiers whose implementation may be asynchronous; that is, the operation may not complete immediately and instead the method may return a job.

properties.description: Enumeration of method identifiers whose implementation may be asynchronous; that is, the operation may not complete immediately and instead the method may return a job.

classes.name: Msvm_VirtualSystemMigrationNetworkSettingData
classes.description: Represents the network on which the virtual system migration service is listening for incoming virtual system migration.

properties.name: SubnetNumber
properties.description: The migration network subnet number.

properties.name: PrefixLength
properties.description: The migration network subnet prefix length.

properties.name: Metric
properties.description: The priority metric of this network for migration. Lower is the value higher is the priority.

properties.name: Tags
properties.description: The array of tags to represent which management system has set this network for virtual system migration service.

classes.name: Msvm_VirtualSystemMigrationService
classes.description: Represents the virtual system migration service. It is used for migrating a virtual system or for migration the storage of a virtual system from one virtualization platform to another.

properties.name: ActiveVirtualSystemMigrationCount
properties.description: The number of currently in-flight virtual system migrations.

properties.name: ActiveStorageMigrationCount
properties.description: The number of currently in-flight storage migrations.

properties.name: MigrationServiceListenerIPAddressList
properties.description: The list of IP addresses of the host, which  can be used for virtual system migration.

methods.name: MigrateVirtualSystemToHost
methods.description: Method to migrate a virtual system or the storage of a virtual system to a destination host specified by a hostname.

parameters.name: ComputerSystem
parameters.description: Source virtual computer system to be migrated.

parameters.name: DestinationHost
parameters.description:
Target host system for the migration.
Acceptable formats for this parameter are conveyed through values of elements of the DestinationHostFormatsSupported[ ] array property in the instance of the CIM_VirtualSystemMigrationCapabilities that is associated through the CIM_ElementCapabilities assocation.

parameters.name: MigrationSettingData
parameters.description: String containing an embedded instance of the CIM_VirtualSystemMigrationSettingData class representing migration settings applicable to the migration operation.

parameters.name: NewSystemSettingData
parameters.description: String containing an embedded instance of the CIM_VirtualSystemSettingData class representing new properties applicable to the virtual system after it is migrated.

parameters.name: NewResourceSettingData
parameters.description: Array of strings each containing an embedded instance of the CIM_ResourceAllocationSettingData class representing new properties applicable to virtual resources in the scope of the virtual system after it is migrated.

parameters.name: Job
parameters.description: If operation is long running then optionally a job may be returned.

methods.name: CheckVirtualSystemIsMigratable
methods.description: Method to migrate a virtual system or the storage of a virtual system to a destination host specified by a hostname.

methods.name: ModifyServiceSettings
methods.description: Modifies the setting data for the service.

methods.name: AddNetworkSettings
methods.description: Adds migration network subnets for virtual system migration service.

methods.name: RemoveNetworkSettings
methods.description: Removes migration network subnets from virtual system migration service.

methods.name: ModifyNetworkSettings
methods.description: Modifies migration network subnets of virtual system migration service.

methods.name: GetSystemCompatibilityInfo
methods.description:
Generates an opaque blob of data containing compatibility information for the specified system.
Remarks: The GetSystemCompatibilityInfo method is used in conjunction with CheckSystemCompatibilityInfo to determine whether a quick or live migration of a VM to another hosting computer system is possible without first trying the migration. The compatibility information is dependent on the current EnabledState property of the system specified in the ComputerSystem parameter. In addition, the compatibility informationfor a VM may change when a snapshot is applied or the system is restarted. A VM in the Disabled state (EnabledState property value of 3) is compatible with more systems than one in the Suspended or Enabled states (EnabledState property value of 32769 or 2).

methods.name: CheckSystemCompatibilityInfo
methods.description: tbd

parameters.name: RequestedState
parameters.description: The state requested for the element. This information will be placed into the RequestedState property of the instance if the return code of the RequestStateChange method is 0 ('Completed with No Error'), or 4096 (0x1000) ('Job Started'). Refer to the description of the EnabledState and RequestedState properties for the detailed explanations of the RequestedState values.

parameters.name: Job
parameters.description: May contain a reference to the ConcreteJob created to track the state transition initiated by the method invocation.

parameters.name: TimeoutPeriod
parameters.description:
A timeout period that specifies the maximum amount of time that the client expects the transition to the new state to take. The interval format must be used to specify the TimeoutPeriod. A value of 0 or a null parameter indicates that the client has no time requirements for the transition. 
If this property does not contain 0 or null and the implementation does not support this parameter, a return code of 'Use Of Timeout Parameter Not Supported' shall be returned.

methods.name: GetSystemCompatibilityVectors
methods.description: Returns a list of Msvm_CompatibilityVector instances that can be used to check for VM to host compatibility.

methods.name: GetProcessorFeatureLimits
methods.description: Returns a list of Msvm_CompatibilityVector instances that can be used to check host compatibility.

methods.name: SetPlannedMigrationSettings
methods.description: Method to configure the migration parameters to be applied to a planned computer system on the migration destination.

parameters.name: PlannedComputerSystem
parameters.description: The planned computer system representing the migration target on the destination host.

parameters.name: MigrationSettingData
parameters.description: String containing an embedded instance of the Msvm_VirtualSystemMigrationSettingData class representing migration settings applicable to the migration operation.

methods.name: QuerySystemMigrationProperties
methods.description: Queries for properties of a virtual machine that affect the migration parameters.

classes.name: Msvm_VirtualSystemMigrationServiceSettingData
classes.description: Represents the settings for the virtual system migration service on a host.The properties for this class cannot be modified directly. The client must call the Msvm_VirtualSystemMigrationService.ModifyServiceSettings method to modify any of these properties.

properties.name: EnableVirtualSystemMigration
properties.description:
Enables or disables virtual system migration. Storage migration is always enabled.
This is a read-only property, but it can be changed using the ModifyServiceSettings method of the Msvm_VirtualSystemMigrationService class.

properties.name: MaximumActiveVirtualSystemMigration
properties.description:
Defines the maximum number of active virtual system migrations allowed.
This is a read-only property, but it can be changed using the ModifyServiceSettings method of the Msvm_VirtualSystemMigrationService class.

properties.name: MaximumActiveStorageMigration
properties.description:
Defines the maximum number of active stroage migrations allowed.
This is a read-only property, but it can be changed using the ModifyServiceSettings method of the Msvm_VirtualSystemMigrationService class.

properties.name: AuthenticationType
properties.description:
Specifies the authentication mechanism used for virtual system migration network connections.
This is a read-only property, but it can be changed using the ModifyServiceSettings method of the Msvm_VirtualSystemMigrationService class.

properties.name: EnableCompression
properties.description:
Enable or disable compression of live migration traffic.
This is a read-only property, but it can be changed using the ModifyServiceSettings method of the Msvm_VirtualSystemMigrationService class.

properties.name: EnableSmbTransport
properties.description:
Enable or disable use of SMB as a transport type for transferring VM state between the Hyper-V hosts during virtual system migration.
This is a read-only property, but it can be changed using the ModifyServiceSettings method of the Msvm_VirtualSystemMigrationService class.

properties.name: MigTdHash
properties.description: Migration TD SHA384 hash.

properties.name: MigTdMappings

qualifiers.Description: JSON string containing mappings of migration policy (e.g. MigTdHash) to a HCS VM ID of the MigTD. The JSON structure looks like this: {"mappings": {"hash1": "HCS ID 1", "hash2": "HCS ID 2"}}. The schema may be extended in future.

properties.description: JSON string containing mappings of migration policy (e.g. MigTdHash) to a HCS VM ID of the MigTD. The JSON structure looks like this: {"mappings": {"hash1": "HCS ID 1", "hash2": "HCS ID 2"}}. The schema may be extended in future.

classes.name: Msvm_VirtualSystemMigrationServiceSettingDataComponent
classes.description: An association used to represent virtual system migration network settings of the virtual system migration service.

properties.name: GroupComponent
properties.description: Reference to an instance of Msvm_VirtualSystemMigrationServiceSettingData representing migration service settings.

properties.name: PartComponent
properties.description: Reference to an instance of Msvm_VirtualSystemMigrationNetworkSettingData representing migration network settings.

classes.name: Msvm_VirtualSystemMigrationSettingData
classes.description:
Represents the migration settings for migrating a virtual system and 
the storage attached to a virtual system.

properties.name: MigrationType
properties.description:
MigrationType describes the type of migration operation to 
be performed.

properties.name: DestinationPlannedVirtualSystemId
properties.description:
If we have a planned computer system at migration destination then DestinationPlannedVirtualSystemId could be set to 
the GUID of the destination Planned VM where the VM needs to 
migrate. This is useful for cases where user has created a 
planned VM at the destination along with resource fixup, and 
wants a VM from source to migrate into this planned VM.

properties.name: DestinationIPAddressList
properties.description:
For storage migration it will be NULL. For virtual system 
migration, client can provide a list of IP addresses of the 
destination host.

properties.name: RetainVhdCopiesOnSource
properties.description:
For storage migration, this could be set to TRUE or FALSE
to determine whether, at the end of a storage migration, when the
source host is beeing clean up, the read only VHDs should be removedor not.

properties.name: EnableCompression
properties.description:
For live migration, this could be set to TRUE or FALSE to determine
whether to compress memory for the live migration traffic or not.

properties.name: TransportType
properties.description:
TransportType indicates the type of transport to be applied for a virtual system migration operation.
- 5(TCP) indicates the TCP transport type.
- 32768(SMB) indicates that the transport type to
be used is SMB for transfer the VM state.

properties.name: UnmanagedVhds
properties.description: An array of embedded Msvm_MoveUnmanagedVhd instances which containunmanaged VHDs information.

properties.name: RemoveSourceUnmanagedVhds
properties.description: Remove source unmanaged VHDs.

properties.name: AllowOverwriteExistingFile
properties.description: Allow the storage migration operation to overwrite existing .vhdx files.

properties.name: AvoidRemovingVHDs
properties.description: Do not remove any VHDs during the migration, i.e. VHDs on the source in successand VHDs on the destination in failure.

properties.name: CancelIfBlackoutThresholdExceeded
properties.description: Cancels migration if the blackout threshold time is exceeded.

properties.name: CPUCappingMagnitude
properties.description: Degree of CPU capping during migration.

properties.name: EnableVhdCompression
properties.description:
For live migration, this could be set to TRUE or FALSE to determine
whether to compress VHD for the live migration traffic or not.

properties.name: AdvancedOptions
properties.description: Advanced options for migration. Optional. Must be a valid JSON, if provided.

properties.name: SecurityContexts
properties.description: Serialized SSPI security contexts.

classes.name: Msvm_VirtualSystemPciExpressSettingData
classes.description: Represents the PCI Express settings that apply to a virtual system.

properties.name: EmulationMode
properties.description: Whether or not to enable emulated PCIe for this virtual system.

properties.name: Topology
properties.description: The PCI Express topology.

classes.name: Msvm_VirtualSystemReferencePoint
classes.description: Represents a reference point for a virtual system.

properties.name: InstanceID

qualifiers.Description: Unique identification of a reference point object.

properties.description: Unique identification of a reference point object.

properties.name: ReferencePointType
properties.description:
Indicates the type of the reference point. 
Log based: Hyper-V replica log tracking. 
RCT based: Based on Resilient Change Tracking of virtual disks.

properties.name: ConsistencyLevel
properties.description:
Consistency level of the reference point.
Crash Consistent: The reference point indicates a point in time when the virtual system was in crash consistent state. 
Application Consistent: The reference point indicates a point in time when the virtual system was in application consistent state.

properties.name: VirtualSystemIdentifier
properties.description: The name of the CIM_ComputerSystem to which this reference point belongs

properties.name: HasAssociatedData
properties.description: This property is set to TRUE if the reference point has associated data. This is valid only for log based reference points. For RCT based reference points this is set to FALSE. For log based reference points, once the log is discarded this value becomes FALSE.

properties.name: VirtualDiskIdentifiers
properties.description: An array of WMI instance IDs corresponding to the virtual disks of the VM at the time this reference point was created. These virtual disks have RCT IDs associated with them.

properties.name: ResilientChangeTrackingIdentifiers
properties.description: An array of Resilient Change Tracking IDs corresponding to the virtual disks of the VM at the time this reference point was created. This field is valid only for RCT based reference points

classes.name: Msvm_VirtualSystemReferencePointExportJob
classes.description: This class represents a virtual system reference point export operation job.

properties.name: Cancellable
properties.description: Indicates whether the job can be cancelled. The value of this property does not guarantee that a request to cancel the job will succeed.

properties.name: ExportDirectory
properties.description: Export Location.

properties.name: VirtualMachineId
properties.description: GUID of the virtual machine for which log files wereexported.

properties.name: ReferencePointId
properties.description: GUID of the reference point which is exported.

properties.name: BaseReferencePointId
properties.description: GUID of the reference point which is used as the base in the exportoperation.

properties.name: ExportedDisks
properties.description: Instance IDs of virtual disks for which log files were exported.

properties.name: ExportedLogFilePaths
properties.description: Paths of the log files which were exported.

properties.name: ExportedConfigFilePath
properties.description: Path of the exported config file.

properties.name: ExportedRuntimeFilePath
properties.description: Path of the exported runtime state file.

properties.name: ExportedGuestStateFilePath
properties.description: Path of the exported guest state file.

parameters.name: RequestedState
parameters.description:
RequestStateChange changes the state of a job. The possible values are as follows: 
Start (2) changes the state to 'Running'. 
Suspend (3) stops the job temporarily. The intention is to subsequently restart the job with 'Start'. It might be possible to enter the 'Service' state while suspended. (This is job-specific.) 
Terminate (4) stops the job cleanly, saving data, preserving the state, and shutting down all underlying processes in an orderly manner. 
Kill (5) terminates the job immediately with no requirement to save data or preserve the state. 
Service (6) puts the job into a vendor-specific service state. It might be possible to restart the job.

parameters.name: TimeoutPeriod
parameters.description:
A timeout period that specifies the maximum amount of time that the client expects the transition to the new state to take. The interval format must be used to specify the TimeoutPeriod. A value of 0 or a null parameter indicates that the client has no time requirements for the transition. 
If this property does not contain 0 or null and the implementation does not support this parameter, a return code of 'Use Of Timeout Parameter Not Supported' must be returned.

parameters.name: Error
parameters.description: If the OperationalStatus on the Job is not "OK", then this method will return a CIM Error instance. Otherwise, when the Job is "OK", null is returned.

methods.name: GetErrorEx
methods.description: When the job is executing or has terminated without error, then this method returns no Msvm_Error instance. However, if the job has failed because of some internal problem or because the job has been terminated by a client, then one or more Msvm_Error instances are returned.
