classes.name: Msvm_VirtualSystemResourceComponent
classes.description: Represents a virtual device component of the Microsoft Windows Hyper-V platform.

properties.name: AdditionalClassNames
properties.description: An array of strings containing additional non-association classes surfaced by this Msvm_VirtualSystemResourceComponent. These classes must neither derive from CIM_LogicalDevice nor CIM_ResourceAllocationSettingData.

properties.name: HotAdd
properties.description: If TRUE, this instance can be hot-added to a VM. The default is FALSE.

properties.name: HotRemove
properties.description: If TRUE, this instance can be hot-removed from a VM. The default is FALSE.

properties.name: Type
properties.description:
The relationship of the WMI object that is described here with the virtual device.
The values for this property are:
Not changeable
Singleton: Singleton is a top level WMI object that is tied 1:1 with a Virtual Device and can only exist once per VM. This is the default value.
MultiInstance: MultiInstance is a top level WMI object that can exist multiple times per VM and is tied 1:1 with a Virtual Device.
Subdevice: Subdevice is a WMI object that has not parent reference but is controlled by only one Virtual Device that can exist only once per VM. The WMI object though can exist multiples times.

properties.name: WmiComponentCLSID
properties.description: A GUID that represents the class identifier of the component's Wmi COM object.

classes.name: Msvm_VirtualSystemResourceRegistration
classes.description: Registers a component that provides virtual system-specific resource-related objects.

properties.name: Component
properties.description: A reference to an instance that describes the COM object that implements this class.

properties.name: IsRootDevice
properties.description: If true, this registration indicates whether the specified resource type is the root (or parent-less) device for this component. Only one registration may exist with IsRootDevice to set true. Otherwise, this registration represents a mapping to a sub-device. This property is always set to false.

classes.name: Msvm_VirtualSystemSettingData
classes.description: Represents the virtualization-specific settings for a virtual system.

properties.name: BIOSGUID
properties.description:
The globally-unique identifier for the BIOS of the virtual computer system.
This is a read-only property, but it can be changed using the ModifyVirtualSystem method of the Msvm_VirtualSystemManagementService class.

properties.name: BIOSSerialNumber
properties.description:
The serial number of the BIOS for the virtual computer system.
This is a read-only property, but it can be changed using the ModifyVirtualSystem method of the Msvm_VirtualSystemManagementService class.

properties.name: BaseBoardSerialNumber
properties.description:
The serial number of the base board for the virtual computer system.
This is a read-only property, but it can be changed using the ModifyVirtualSystem method of the Msvm_VirtualSystemManagementService class.

properties.name: ChassisSerialNumber
properties.description:
The serial number of the chassis for the virtual computer system.
This is a read-only property, but it can be changed using the ModifyVirtualSystem method of the Msvm_VirtualSystemManagementService class.

properties.name: ChassisAssetTag
properties.description:
The asset tag of the chassis for the virtual computer system.
This is a read-only property, but it can be changed using the ModifyVirtualSystem method of the Msvm_VirtualSystemManagementService class.

properties.name: BIOSNumLock
properties.description:
This property is set to TRUE if the num lock key is set to on by the BIOS, FALSE if the num lock key is set to off by the BIOS.
This is a read-only property, but it can be changed using the ModifyVirtualSystem method of the Msvm_VirtualSystemManagementService class.

properties.name: BootOrder
properties.description:
The boot order set within the BIOS of the virtual computer system. This property is an array of values, max length 4, where each value indicates a device to boot from. The virtual computer system will first attempt to boot from the device indicated by the first value within the array. If that device does not contain a boot sector, the virtual computer system will attempt to boot from the next device specified by the BootOrder property and so on. If no device specified within the BootOrder contains a boot sector the virtual computer system will fail to boot. The default value for a virtual computer system is [0, 1, 2, 3, 4].
Value definitions:
0 (Floppy): The virtual computer system will attempt to boot from the floppy disk within the floppy drive.
1 (CD-ROM): The virtual computer system will attempt to boot from the first CD or DVD disk found with a boot sector.
2 (IDE Hard Drive): The virtual computer system will attempt to boot from the first hard drive found attached to an IDE controller with a boot sector.
3 (PXE Boot): The virtual computer system will attempt to PXE boot from the network.
4 (SCSI Hard Drive): The virtual computer system will attempt to boot from the first hard drive found attached to a SCSI controller with a boot sector.
5-65535: Reserved
This is a read-only property, but it can be changed using the ModifyVirtualSystem method of the Msvm_VirtualSystemManagementService class.

properties.name: Parent
properties.description: The object path for the snapshot Msvm_VirtualSystemSettingData from which this object is based. This property will be NULL if this object is not based off a snapshot.

properties.name: VirtualSystemIdentifier
properties.description: The name of the CIM_ComputerSystem to which this setting data belongs

properties.name: IsSaved
properties.description:
This property is set to TRUE if the configuration has a reference to a saved state file, FALSE if not. Note that this does not indicate the presence of such a file, only that the configuration specifies one.
This is a read-only property, it cannot be changed.

properties.name: AdditionalRecoveryInformation
properties.description:
Any additional information provided to the recovery action. The meaning of this property is defined by the action in AutomaticRecoveryAction. If AutomaticRecoveryAction is 0 ("None") or 1 ("Restart"), this value is NULL. If AutomaticRecoveryAction is 2 ("Revert to Snapshot"), this is the object path to a snapshot that should be applied on failure of the virtual machine worker process.
This is a read-only property, but it can be changed using the ModifyVirtualSystem method of the Msvm_VirtualSystemManagementService class.

properties.name: AllowFullSCSICommandSet
properties.description:
Indicates whether SCSI commands from the guest operating system are passed to pass-through disks. If TRUE, SCSI commands emitted by the guest operating system to pass-through disks are not filtered.It is recommended that SCSI filtering remains enabled for production deployments.
This is a read-only property, but it can be changed using the ModifyVirtualSystem method of the Msvm_VirtualSystemManagementService class.
Windows Server 2008:  The AllowFullSCSICommandSet property is not supported.

properties.name: DebugChannelId
properties.description: The channel identifier used to debug the virtual system using the VUD unified debugger.

properties.name: DebugPortEnabled
properties.description: Whether the virtual system is using synthetic debugging.

properties.name: DebugPort
properties.description: The tcpip port used to debug the virtual system using synthetic debugging.

properties.name: Version
properties.description:
The version of the virtual system in a format of "major.minor", for example "2.0".
Windows Server 2008:  The Version property is not supported.

properties.name: IncrementalBackupEnabled
properties.description:
Indicates whether the Hyper-V VSS writer supports taking incremental backup of this Virtual machine.
This is a read-write property.

properties.name: VirtualNumaEnabled
properties.description: Indicates whether virtual non-uniform memory access (NUMA) nodes are projected into the virtual machine. If FALSE, the virtual machine will have a single node. If TRUE, the number of virtual NUMA nodes projected into the virtual machine is determined from the values of the Msvm_ProcessorSettingData.MaxProcessorsPerNumaNode and Msvm_MemorySettingData.MaxMemoryBlocksPerNumaNode properties.

properties.name: AllowReducedFcRedundancy
properties.description:
Indicates whether live migration of a virtual machine that is configured with a virtual FC adapter is allowed to a destination machine, without doing any checks for the existence of paths to the storage devices on the destination 
The default value of this property is FALSE. If set to TRUE, the VM can be live migrated to a target machine which may have no or reduced paths to the target FC devices. The guest operating system may lose connectivity to storage and may behave in an unpredictable manner. 
 This property should be cleared after a live migration

properties.name: BootSourceOrder
properties.description: Contains references to the boot source entries of this virtual computer system. This property is an array of strings, where each string is a full path to a Msvm_BootSourceSettingData instance indicating a device, file, or other boot source to boot from.

properties.name: LowMmioGapSize
properties.description: The size of the Low (below 4GB) Memory-Mapped IO Gap in MB

properties.name: VirtualSystemSubType
properties.description: The subtype of the virtual system.

properties.name: SecureBootEnabled
properties.description: Secure boot enabled setting for the VM

properties.name: PauseAfterBootFailure
properties.description: Pause after boot failure setting for the VM

properties.name: NetworkBootPreferredProtocol
properties.description: Identifies the IP protocol used for network boot.

properties.name: UserSnapshotType
properties.description:
Indicates the user-defined snapshot type. 
Disable: Disable the creation of any snapshot. 
ProductionFallbackToTest: Data-consistent snapshot for use in the production environment.Performs a snapshot with application state when the ability to create data consistent snapshot is not available. 
ProductionNoFallback: Data-consistent snapshot for use in the production environment.Does not create a snapshot with application state if it is not possible to create a data consistent snapshot. 
Test: Snapshot that contains memory and device information for test and development purpose.

properties.name: SecureBootTemplateId
properties.description:
The globally-unique identifier of the template of intial values of UEFI Secure Boot related variables.
This is a read-only property, but it can be changed using the ModifyVirtualSystem method of the Msvm_VirtualSystemManagementService class.

properties.name: ConsoleMode
properties.description: Identifies the Console Mode for the VM

properties.name: AutomaticCriticalErrorAction
properties.description: Identifies the action to be taken on VM, when a critical error happens, like storage disconnect.The value of 1, causes the VM to be paused and automatically resumed when the critical error condition is resolved. This behavior can be overridden by setting the value to 0 upon which no specific action will be taken for critical error conditions.

properties.name: AutomaticCriticalErrorActionTimeout
properties.description: Identifies the maximum duration for which the AutomaticCriticalErrorAction will be performed to resolve the critical error. This is applicable only when the value of the AutomaticCriticalErrorAction property is not 0 (None). Once the timeout expires, the VM will be powered off.The value will be rounded up to the nearest minute. A value of 0 implies that the VM should be powered off immediately when it encounters a critical error condition.

properties.name: ParentPackage
properties.description: If this system is a container, the path to the Msvm_ContainerPackage from which this system is based.

properties.name: LockOnDisconnect
properties.description: Lock the console when disconnecting from vmconnect.

properties.name: GuestControlledCacheTypes
properties.description: Indicates whether the guest can control cache types.

properties.name: HighMmioGapSize
properties.description: The size of the High (above 4GB) Memory-Mapped IO Gap in MB

properties.name: ParentContainerImage
properties.description: If this system is a container, the path to the Msvm_ContainerImage from which this system is based.

properties.name: ContainerRuntimeType
properties.description: Specifies the runtime environment for a container virtual system.

properties.name: Architecture
properties.description: The architecture of this system.

properties.name: GuestStateDataRoot

qualifiers.Description: Filepath of a directory where information about the guest runtime state is stored.

properties.description: Filepath of a directory where information about the guest runtime state is stored.

properties.name: GuestStateFile

qualifiers.Description: Filepath of a file where information about the guest runtime state is stored. A relative path appends to the value of the GuestStateDataRoot property.

properties.description: Filepath of a file where information about the guest runtime state is stored. A relative path appends to the value of the GuestStateDataRoot property.

properties.name: IsAutomaticSnapshot
properties.description: Indicates whether this is a snapshot created automatically for the user.

properties.name: AutomaticSnapshotsEnabled
properties.description: Indicates whether this virtual machine should have automatic snapshots enabled.

properties.name: HighMmioGapBase
properties.description: The base address of the High (above 4GB) Memory-Mapped IO Gap in MB

properties.name: EnhancedSessionTransportType
properties.description: Indicates the transport type to use when connecting to an enhanced session.

properties.name: EnableHibernation
properties.description:
This property is set to TRUE if hibernate (S4) is enabled by the BIOS, FALSE if hibernate is disabled.
This is a read-only property, but it can be changed using the ModifyVirtualSystem method of the Msvm_VirtualSystemManagementService class.

properties.name: TurnOffOnGuestRestart
properties.description: Turn off the VM instead of restarting the VM if the guest operating system initiated a restart.

properties.name: BootPciExpress
properties.description: If set to true, the virtual system will attempt to boot from virtual PCI Express devices if no successful boot source entry is found.

properties.name: BootPciExpressInstanceFilter
properties.description: If set, the device instance GUID of the root virtual PCI Express controller to boot devices from.

properties.name: NumaNodeTopologyArray
properties.description: An array of embedded Msvm_NumaNodeTopology instances which contains NUMA node topology for each NUMA node.

properties.name: GuestStateIsolationEnabled
properties.description: Indicates whether the virtual system should have guest state isolation enabled.

properties.name: VirtualSlitType
properties.description: Indicates whether the virtual system should have virtual SLIT enabled and the type of measurements used to generate the virtual SLIT.

properties.name: GuestStateIsolationType
properties.description: Indicates the isolation type of the virtual system.

properties.name: ClusterWideNodeCapabilitiesValidationMode
properties.description: Indicates if the validation logic to check if features are supported cluster wide needs to be overridden or not.

properties.name: Vtl2AddressRangeBase
properties.description: The base address of the VTL2 address space range in MB

properties.name: Vtl2AddressRangeSize
properties.description: The size of the VTL2 address space range in MB

properties.name: Vtl2MmioAddressRangeSize
properties.description: The size of the VTL2 Memory-Mapped IO address  space range in MB

properties.name: SourceGuestStateFile

qualifiers.Description: Filepath to a source file that will be copied and used as the guest runtime state.

properties.description: Filepath to a source file that will be copied and used as the guest runtime state.

properties.name: Vtl2AddressSpaceConfigurationMode
properties.description: Indicates VTL2 address space configuration mode.

properties.name: GuestStateIsolationMode
properties.description: Indicates the isolation mode of the virtual system.

properties.name: VMBusMessageRedirection
properties.description: Indicates whether to redirect VMBus control plane messages.

properties.name: WorkerJobObjectName
properties.description: Name of the job object to start the VM worker process in.

properties.name: MemoryHostingJobObjectName
properties.description: Name of the job object to start the VM memory process in.

properties.name: FirmwareFile
properties.description: Firmware file.

properties.name: FirmwareParameters
properties.description: Parameters provided to the firmware file.

properties.name: WatchdogEnabled
properties.description: Indicates if the virtual system should expose an emulated hardware watchdog.

properties.name: GuestFeatureSet
properties.description: Flags to modify guest-visible behaviors

properties.name: ManagementVtlUpdatePolicy
properties.description:
The policy for performing updates of the management VTL of this virtual machine. 
OfflineOnly: Do not reload the management VTL while running. Updates will be applied on next restart.

properties.name: DefaultBootAlwaysAttempt
properties.description: If set to true, the virtual system attempt a default boot even if boot entries exist and fail.

properties.name: Vtl2ReservedDmaSizeMB
properties.description: The portion of the VTL2 memory which should be preserved during servicing operation

properties.name: VirtualHmatType
properties.description: Indicates virtual HMAT configuration mode.

properties.name: GuestStateLifetime
properties.description: Indicates the lifetime of the virtual machine guest state.

properties.name: LaunchData
properties.description: Launch data of isolated VM.

properties.name: VMBusHybridHvSocketPath
properties.description: Path on the host for the UNIX socket path for the Hybrid HvSocket relay. Disabled if empty.

properties.name: VMBusManagementVtlHybridHvSocketPath
properties.description: Path on the host for the UNIX socket path for the Management VTL Hybrid HvSocket relay. Disabled if empty.

properties.name: ResourcePartitionID

properties.name: ManagementVtlAutomaticUpdateFlags
properties.description: The flags to pass to the automatic managment VTL update operation. Only applies if automatic management VTL update is enabled.

properties.name: MaximumHierarchicalPartitions
properties.description: Maximum number of child partitions that a hierarchical virtualization partition can create.

properties.name: MaximumLayeredPartitions
properties.description: Maximum number of child partitions that a layered virtualization partition can create.

properties.name: PartitionDiagnosticBufferCount
properties.description: The number of hypervisor tracing buffers that a layered virtualization partition uses.

properties.name: PartitionDiagnosticBufferSizeInPages
properties.description: The size, in pages, of each hypervisor tracing buffer that a layered virtualization partition uses.

properties.name: BootEmulatedPciExpress
properties.description: If set to true, the virtual system will attempt to boot from emulated PCI Express devices if no successful boot source entry is found.

properties.name: EfiDiagnosticsLogLevel
properties.description: The log level for EfiDiagnostics.

properties.name: ManagementVtlFeatureFlags
properties.description: Flags to modify management VTL feature behaviors

properties.name: GuestStateEncryptionPolicy
properties.description: The persistent state encryption policy for the management VTL.

properties.name: EnableLayeredIntegratedScheduler
properties.description: Indicates whether Hyper-V should enable integrated scheduler for the layered VM.

properties.name: EnableVirtualIommu
properties.description: Indicates whether Hyper-V should enable virtual IOMMU for the VM.

properties.name: Component

properties.name: ResourceType

classes.name: Msvm_WiFiDeviceSAPImplementation
classes.description: An association between a service access point (SAP) and how it is implemented. The cardinality of this association is many-to-many. A SAP can be provided by more than one logical device, operating in conjunction. Any device can provide more than one SAP. When many logical devices are associated with a single SAP, it is assumed that these elements operate in conjunction to provide the access point. If different implementations of a SAP exist, each of these implementations would result in individual instantiations of the SAP object. These individual instantiations would then have associations to the unique implementations.

classes.name: Msvm_WiFiEndpoint
classes.description: Represents the logical connection point for a network adapter. When the WiFi endpoint is connected to a switch port, the network adapter connected to the WiFi endpoint has network connectivity.

properties.name: Connected
properties.description: This property is set to TRUE if the WiFi endpoint is connected to a switch port.

classes.name: Msvm_WiFiPort
classes.description: Represents a physical WiFi (802.11) network adapter that can be bound to a virtual switch to provide external network connectivity to virtual machines.

properties.name: IsBound
properties.description: If this property is true, then this WiFi port can be connected to the switches and thus can provide connectivity to virtual machine. If this property is false, then this WiFi is not being used by the virtual machine networking architecture.
